Loved this salad. Doubled it for a crowd. Used apple juice and had no trouble with the farro being undercooked. Just taste it. Added additional crunch vegetables, like peas from the garden. The textures were wonderful. Use a good Parmesan cheese. I reserved a little to see what it would be like the next day and it was just as wonderful. Oh, and I toasted the pistachios. I do not know if my farro is pearled or not: it is sold in my local supermarket from a company called Pereg.
An excellent coleslaw. Can be made ahead and will stay crisp and bright. The 2 cabbages make it very pretty. Just keep adding color. I will make this many times this summer.

comments.recipe_name: whiskey soaked dark chocolate bundt cake
comments.comment: This is a fantastic cake. I've made it a number of times and it is always a crowd pleaser. So moist and it can be made ahead of the party. This time I only had 3 oz of unsweetened chocolate (Scharffen Berger) so I used 2 oz of a bittersweet chocolate and cut the amount of sugar by a couple of tablespoons. This cake is easy (butter your Bundt pan well), quite nice to look at, and delicious. A winner!

comments.recipe_name: steamed or roasted beets and beet greens with tahini sauce
comments.comment: Excellent. I too usually roast my beets. They steamed in less time than I thought they would, so check them after a half hour. You don't want them too soft. I made double the amount of beets but the same amount of dressing in the recipe. I like salads underdressed. This is a great way to use beets from the garden as you use the greens also.

comments.recipe_name: cucumber salad with soy ginger and garlic
comments.comment: It reminded me of a dressing for cold noodles so I spiraled a zucchini into it. Yummy.
Nice, light salad. The couscous is a little soft.
We did a whole cut-up Griggstown chicken give or take this way. Chicken was juicy, flavor was wonderful. Use a good chicken; work your grill.

Excellent. While not a fan of jarred barbecue sauces, this home-made version was perfect. If you don't like sweet, use a scant 1/4 cup light brown sugar as I did. The smoked paprika makes it. I diluted a little for basting, not much.

Thick and rich but light at the same time. I used diluted turkey stock from last Thanksgiving and what seemed like a ton of chard, which hardly made a dent in the garden. I chose not to use lemon but think the Parmesan added depth to this lovely soup. Serve either hot or lukewarm.

comments.recipe_name: rice pilaf with carrots and parsley
comments.comment: It was too hot to pick the parsley so we forgot that. With local leeks and multicolored carrots. this was both delicious and beautiful. So sweet that it tasted like it had honey in it.

comments.recipe_name: grilled clams and mussels with garlic almonds and mint
comments.comment: We brought the ingredients for this wonderful dish to a lakeside barbecue. What a perfect summer dish. Good bread is essential. Next time I might substitute tarragon or basil for the mint or in addition to the mint and add more red pepper. Eating grilled clams and mussels outside, this is what we live for.

This recipe works! It's very tasty and I don't feel guilty eating it. I grate the corn over a cookie sheet and saute a hot green pepper with the onion. This time of year we have so much fresh corn and this is a great way to serve it as an accompaniment to a nice dinner.

comments.recipe_name: roasted beets with pine nuts and parmesan
comments.comment: Perfect for fresh beets. The flavor just jumps out of each bite. I toasted pine nuts (probably used 1/4 cup), used a whole lemon, and added salt. Wonderful!

comments.recipe_name: pearl couscous with sauteed cherry tomatoes
comments.comment: A wonderful way to use my cherry tomatoes that are taking over the kitchen. I added corn (2 ears microwaved and kernels cut off the cobs) to the mix. The corn provided the sweetness, so I did not use the sugar, and what was most surprising, was indistinguishable from the couscous. Delicious side dish on a summer's night.

This is the best fried chicken and the easiest. I've made it many times and I always love it. I usually use canola oil but it works with corn oil and all the vegetable oil blends. I added a little duck fat (instead of bacon drippings) and I think it adds a subtle richness. Just a wonderful recipe.

Perfect summer side dish. I used the medium tomatoes as directed, and added a bit of sriracha (maybe a bit too much) to spice it up more. The tomatoes stayed together beautifully. I used my own no-knead bread (2 days old) for the crumbs so except for the cheese and salt and pepper, it was all home-grown.

What a wonderful inspiration. Just pick a few beautiful tomatoes (different colors are nice), slice them thick (make sure your knife is sharp), and throw the cherry tomatoes with in the vinaigrette on top. Add (or don't add) anchovies, basil, capers, and olives, and voila: you are in France. Gorgeous to look at on a large platter; make it your own!

comments.recipe_name: stuffed peppers with red rice chard and feta
comments.comment: I made this with just-picked green peppers and Swiss chard and used red quinoa instead of rice. Delicious side. Still good the next day reheated in the microwave. I did not use the tomato paste but if I had an tomato sauce around I might have used that.

Delicious! I baked it and brought it to a party. I used a mix of Gruyere and a sharp Cheddar (that's what I had on hand) and just black olives (they were nicoise olives, which I had to carefully pit). Very nice.

comments.recipe_name: apple kuchen with honey and ginger
comments.comment: This is a wonderful recipe. The cake looked good and tasted wonderful. I used tart green apples and crystallized ginger and baked it in a 9" springform pan, as recipe recommends. This is one time I wouldn't change a thing.

Perfection. I messed the torte's appearance up a bit by attempting (unsuccessfully) to remove it from the bottom of the baking pan so I just threw some confectioner's sugar on top. No harm done. It looked great and tasted even better.
Easy to make, nice to look at. But I just don't care for the flavor.

comments.recipe_name: tomatoes stuffed with bulgur and herbs
comments.comment: A nice way to use a few extra tomatoes. Tasty, light, sort of homey, a great side for beef. My tomato tops weren't so I didn't put them back on (I just covered all with foil) and I skipped step #4 all together. You will need to carefully pour the liquid out of the baking dish.

Very nice. Not a standard borscht but something else. The mushroom broth give it an earthiness and the sliced mushrooms gives it a meatiness, both of which I enjoyed. My beet greens didn't look so good so I used Swiss chard instead. I did like the yogurt in it. This was a tasty soup.

This cake was too soft, maybe due to the no-stick surface of the bundt pan. I'm really not sure of why. It's very moist, nice flavor, but just a little too crumbly. I'm tasting it again, now on day 2, and it is better. Perhaps it just needed more time to sit. So make ahead.

Excellent basic recipe. My favorite meats for meatballs are lamb and buffalo. I add whatever else is around. Sometimes I use flavored breadcrumb; sometimes I add grated cheese; I'll use any mix of spices that I'm in the mood for. I cook meatballs on the stove. My hint is to make sure you put them into hot oil and then don't turn them or shake the pan until the meat is set, so that the meatball does not fall apart.

comments.recipe_name: chickpea stew with orzo and mustard greens
comments.comment: This dish is delicious. It just happened that my early fall CSA allotment contained so many of these vegetables: fennel, carrots, cherry tomatoes, garlic, onion, and bitter greens and kale. I got to use them all and I'm sure that the freshness of the ingredients had something to do with the deliciousness of the dish. This would be an excellent vegetarian dish for a party (I used water). My only suggestion is that if used it as a side dish, cut the water to 3 cups, so it isn't as soupy.

comments.recipe_name: maureen aboods eggplant with lamb tomato and pine nuts
comments.comment: Delicious! I found the cooking instructions perfect, no problem with the timing. I strongly suggest having the 3 layers of eggplant (for the drama) so if you find yourself without enough eggplant slices, just downsize your baking dish a bit. Local lamb is best as it usually has less fat and it never hurts to add more garlic.
Works like a charm. Much much better than any bread in my neighborhood (I am not in the city). Can anything be easier? Smells like bread, feels like bread, tastes like bread, and it's magic.

It's almost not fair for me to rate because I do so love celery root. I used 2% milk and maybe 1/3 a stick of butter and with about 1/2 lb celery root and 1 1/2 lbs potatoes.Served with a rack of lamb. Delicious!!!
A wonderful side dish. And I make risottos frequently. Fresh parsnips and kale. Almost sweet and bitter. I didn't bother to add the cheese; the flavor without it was wonderful!

comments.recipe_name: moroccan style cornish hens with couscous
comments.comment: Lovely dish with for poussins. Spatchcocked them (instead of halving) and cooked them on the grill. Be sure to make plenty of couscous (Israeli).

comments.recipe_name: oil free spinach with ginger and garlic
comments.comment: Simplicity in itself and perfect. Spinach retains its sparkle.

comments.recipe_name: golden beet and beet greens salad with yogurt mint and dill
comments.comment: A knock-out of a vegetable dish. And I like that it can be prepared in advance and then assembled last minute. I used a combination of red and yellow beets (the yellow ones cook faster but it didn't matter), a variety of greens, and low-fat Greek yogurt. The coriander-cumin mix is essential; what a wonderful flavor the spices impart.

comments.recipe_name: red cabbage and black rice greek style
comments.comment: The prepared dish looks good, is moist. and is interestingly textured. However, it lacked flavor. The cabbage is lost and I wanted it to assert is presence.

comments.recipe_name: stir fried brussels sprouts with shallots and sherry
comments.comment: I'm experimenting with Brussels sprouts recipes for Thanksgiving that can be made ahead and reheated. I think this one will be the winner. Moist and tasty, not mushy. Very nice. If you are doing in am for serving several hours later, I recommend not keeping the dish covered after cooking. That will turn them mushy. Cool first and then cover and put in refrigerator if you must.

comments.recipe_name: spicy stir fried collard greens with red or green cabbage
comments.comment: Excellent side dish and a good way to use collards. Very nice flavors and textures.
Delicious and easy (as long as you have good swordfish). Served with a fennel-leek risotto (no cheese) and a light salad. Swordfish was oh so tender.

comments.recipe_name: stir fried turkey and brussels sprouts
comments.comment: An excellent post-Thanksgiving dinner. Served over sushi rice in a bowl. Subbed shallots for scallions. Very tasty. After stir-frying, I put lid on wok for a minute or two.

The comments here are instructive as to why we should be measuring in grams. This dish was wonderful. I used 1 cup of Arborio rice (1/2 lb) and cut most of the ingredients in half except for the squash (delicata) and the saffron. I used turkey stock. It turned out delicious, with a sofrito-like crust. I mixed the ingredients in the pan I had fried the onions in and then placed it in the oven, baked for 20 minutes, and then covered the dish and turned off the oven as I finished preparing dinner.

I love this recipe. I made it with whole legs (slit between the thigh and drumstick) and it tasted like pieces from a roast chicken. It's not exactly crisp, but it's crispish and the gravy it generates is wonderful. In answer to the query below, I would pair it with a relatively simply pilaf with complementary flavors, rice and lentils would be nice, but check the seasonings or change chicken flavoring a bit.

Ah, the aromas taking this dish out of the oven. This is a great potato dish. It can be made earlier in the day and then roasted (step 3) right before dinner. I cut the fat off of the duck, melted it down, and did add a little olive oil just before roasting. I doubled the recipe and used a lot of garlic and served it with a roast duck. No reason not to use chicken fat and put the cracklings on top.

Put aside all your ideas about what your grandmother's kugel. This is an excellent recipe as written and an elegant side dish to a roast or a stew. I pretty much followed the recipe (I did cover it with tin foil for the last 30 minutes and wouldn't have mentioned it if it weren't for the previous comment).. I grated the potatoes and onion in my Cuisinart, which I don't do for latkes, but it worked perfectly here, no extra squeezing or draining was necessary. Delicous.

This was amazingly good.I prepared it yesterday and served it today as suggested and served it with the potato kugel recipe from Francis Lam on the NYT website. This may be the best beef stew I've ever made. It may be that the olives added a complexity; it may have been the vegetable mix ( of which I couldn't discern individual flavors), but whatever, it was truly wonderfully fragrant. The meat was very tender, the tomatoes in winter are remarkable like this. Thanks, Florence

I've made many banana breads over the years and this is one of the best. I just happened to have 5 frozen bananas. I let them defrost while my eggs and butter came to room temperature. The banana bread was ready in 55 minutes, not 65. It was so light that it was almost spongy and the aroma of the banana permeated the cake. I think the topping really adds to it, in both appearance and taste. Also, it gets better on day 2 and perhaps day 3, but it was eaten up.

Delicious. I used halibut. The spices were there but not overpowering. I served the fish with a quinoa-egg noodle combo and the sauce and onions just almost melted all together. Very different but not strange.

comments.recipe_name: lamb necks braised in wine with peppers
comments.comment: OMG. If you can get a hold of some lamb necks, you must try this. I had 2 necks but they were not broken down so I was worried about cooking them whole. I marinated them basically as is in the recipe, cooking the onions and cooking down the marinade. I put the lamb in the pot and cooked for maybe 4 hours, turning now and then. I cooled the pot overnight and skimmed the fat before reheating with the peppers and also, mashed potatoes. Amazing. The meat just fell off the bone. And the flavors...

The rub really flavored the chops. I left it on about 8 hours. The sauce wasn't essential (I used sour cream instead of creme fraiche but I doubt that mattered much) as the chops were flavorful and juicy. next time just a little broth and the shallots for the sauce.
Very simple side and nice use of butternut squash. It brought out all the natural sweetness. I used it alongside lamb chops.

comments.recipe_name: roasted turkey drumsticks with star anise and soy sauce
comments.comment: Haven't done a dinner of turkey legs in years. Really good. Enjoyed the marinade. The turkey looks great as the marinade caramelizes on the skin and the meat was very tender and tasty. And then the marinade is good with rice,mashed potatoes, etc.

This is my first time making this. I have made the 6-hour version frequently. This is phenomenal: the quicker one is wonderful, this one is even more wonderful. I used 1 1/2 cup regular unbleached flour and 1 1/2 cup bread flour, a generous 1/4 tsp yeast, and next time I think I'll only use 1 1/2 cups water. The dough was very sticky and I didn't think it would work but wow! it sure did. A 4-qt. Staub pot (I used cornmeal) cooked it perfectly, the crust was the essence of a crust.

I really liked this, something different, not a meatloaf, not a burger, something else. The crunch is great. I used ground lamb and served it with mashed potatoes, roasted beets, and sour cream. To make fine-grain bulgur, take any bulgur and put in the food processor for a few pulses.

comments.recipe_name: tuna au poivre with red wine sauce
comments.comment: Superb. The steaks were a bit thicker so I seared on all sides (and my husband doesn't like it too rare). I suggest coating steaks well with spice mixture (I probably used close to 1/2 tsp each of fennel and coriander for 2 steaks). Flavor was fantastic. Served with mashed potatoes and steamed broccoli. Red wine and fish go well together.

Delicious. I used this dish as a side to roast chicken. I only made 1/2 lb of penne and used 6 oz portobellos, sliced thin, dried porcini, and a small can (13 oz?) of diced tomatoes. It needed liquid so I added porcini water and some pasta water. Excellent dish.

Very yummy. Interesting in that it uses white wine and vermouth, not a hearty red wine. Yet it works beautifully. I didn't have the herbs for the garnish but did add shallots in a little bit of vinegar and a tiny bit of honey. Not necessary but it worked too. Wonderful on an evening when the temperature is expected to go below 0.
I used kohlrabi and turnips. It's good but not my favorite. It be good as sort of amuse bouche--when a fancy restaurant puts a small amount of a thick soup in a glass--before a meal.

comments.recipe_name: whole roasted cauliflower with almond herb sauce
comments.comment: An ingenious way to cook a cauliflower! Looked beautiful (after about 55 minutes in the oven), sliced nicely, and the sauce was quite wonderful. For the last year I have been microwaving cauliflower (I learned that technique from a hot-shot chef) but I think this was even better. I just used parsley but I can't wait until summer when I can play with different fresh herbs in the sauce.

My carrots and parsnips weren't small--they were large specimens from my winter CSA--but they are sweet. I peeled and cut each one into maybe 16 pieces (I used 2 carrots and 2 parsnips), put them flat into a frying pan as directed with some water and maybe 1 1/2 TBSP butter, and simmered. After about 15 minutes I noticed that the ones closer to the middle of the pan were almost soft and the ones around the perimeter needed more time. So I stirred and in about 10 minutes they were beautiful.

comments.recipe_name: beet and radicchio salad with goat cheese and pistachios
comments.comment: The parts didn't quite add up to the whole, i.e., the beets were so wonderful by themselves and they were quite muted in the salad. Salad looked beautiful but needed something. I'll work on it.

Many steps but easy. Just don't rush it. I cut the recipe in half, using one large drumstick, just a little bacon (smoked applewood bacon, regular cut), venison sausage, a mix of panko and whole wheat matza meal for the topping, and probably some other substitutions, additions or subtractions. And I let the concoction rest for a day in the refrigerator. Days 3 and 4 we had the most delicious dinner, with just a salad on the side. Rich, aromatic, like a true cassoulet. A knockout dish.

So easy and so good. A warm cocktail sauce is wonderful. Who knew? And roasting the shrimp instead of boiling...luscious. This is an easy appetizer that is sure to please. You flavor the sauce according to your audience but so much better than commercial sauces.

comments.recipe_name: baked chicken with potatoes cherry tomatoes and herbs
comments.comment: Excellent dish and very pretty presentation. I used a whole, cut-up chicken and pretty much followed the recipe as written, using an 11" round Dutch oven. The potatoes did not cook evenly for some reason but everything else worked. I'm not sure that the basil really added anything except some color at the end; parsley would do the same. And I would also add a few cloves of garlic to the mix. Baking instructions were spot on for me: 450 for 30 minutes and then convection 475 for 15.

Excellent way to use all the kale I get in my CSA package. Very nice side dish. I used wine for my liquid. That splash of vinegar at the end just brightened the flavors up. For someone who is so over kale, this was a good and simple recipe that I will come back to.

comments.recipe_name: craig claibornes smothered chicken with mushrooms
comments.comment: Delicious. I used chicken thighs, cut-up onions, and wonderful mushrooms. I served it with mushroom ravioli and used the sauce over the ravioli and chicken. I thickened the sauce up, just a bit, with Wondra flour. Just delicious. I wish I could tell Craig Claiborne just how much I learned from him.

comments.recipe_name: chicken breast milanese with green olive celery relish
comments.comment: I just happened to have pheasant breasts. I soaked them in the buttermilk and breaded as recommended and then fried in corn oil. Really good. I quite liked the way to test if the oil is at the right temperature. I stuck my Thermapen in just to make sure and it was right on.

Couldn't be easier. I basically emptied my vegetable drawer: carrots, parsnips, turnips, celery root, onions, shallots, tossed them with the olive oil, turned at 20 minutes and added thyme, and cooked for another 20 minutes. Then let them sit in cooling down oven until ready to serve.
very nice. Used my first crop of asparagus. Cut them to fit the pan. Butter and herbs work beautifully. I will use ends for a soup.

I made this using a 9 1/2 inch deep dish pie plate. Looked gorgeous. I made it with left-over guinea hen and wild mushrooms and dish was delicious. The biscuit-like topping is very nice, light, attractive, and tasty. So much better to make your own than to use Bisquick. I added the guinea hen at the same time as the peas as it was already cooked but I needed it to bind with the rest of the ingredients.

comments.recipe_name: marinated celery salad with chickpeas and parmesan
comments.comment: Delicious salad. Would be a wonderful summer dish at a barbecue but also good on this wintry spring day. I did not have fresh basil so I used a dried herb combo and I did not make croutons. I loved the crunch of the celery. If using canned chickpeas, always strain and rinse.
Wonderful when the asparagus and the garlic are fresh (and from the vegetable garden). Very nice flavor but this is not the most attractive way to serve asparagus.
Very, very good. A nice spring salad. I suggest making the salad at least a few hours in advance, refrigerating, tossing, and then tasting for seasoning.

comments.recipe_name: pan roasted green beans with golden almonds
comments.comment: Quite nice charring the beans like this. It is, however, too easy to burn the nuts so keep your eyes on them. I liked that I could prepare the beans through step 1 and the nuts in step 2, and then finish the dish just before serving dinner.

comments.recipe_name: grilled roast chicken with spinach ricotta crostini
comments.comment: Excellent! I always miss the drippings when we cook chicken on the grill so this really worked for me. The spinach was to die for. I only had time for the rub to marinate for an hour (basically the time it took to heat the grill and pan) but it did its job. The chicken was excellent, moist and flavorful. It took about 50 minutes to cook. I didn't use the ricotta but the grilled bread soaked up the jus on the serving platter. This and a salad was a perfect spring dinner on the porch.

comments.recipe_name: braised halibut with asparagus baby potatoes and saffron
comments.comment: Fantastic spring dish. I had no problem with the fish. I heated my All-Clad frying pan with olive oil to cover the bottom of the pan to sizzle. My fillets were thick so I seared all 4 sides. I cut the recipe in half for 2 of us but used the full amount of water. My asparagus were right out of the garden as was the garlic. Lovely flavor, very filling. I love dinners that are cooked in one pot. This was an elegant dinner that could be served at the finest of restaurants.

I too greatly increased the amount of kimchi (from my CSA). The potato salad was great; the next day it was milder so I added a little more kimchi. This is one of those recipes where amounts are very fluid depending on how spicy you like things (and, of course, the heat of your kimchi) but it's a lovely break from a traditional potato salad.

comments.recipe_name: yotam ottolenghis tomato and pomegranate salad
comments.comment: This is a lovely salad. I used a good quality feta and pomegranate seeds that are sold in a cuplike container in my supermarket in New Jersey, King's. I served it as a side salad with grilled meat and I think it would have been better as its own course. The salad is simple to prepare and lovely to look at but the flavors are big (or at least different) and you don't want it to get lost among other sides. As tomatoes come in later this summer, I imagine this will be served frequently.

This was excellent, quick, attractive, easy to transport, can keep at room temperature for hours, and versatile. I added chives and used a mixture of potatoes. The trick is to not overcook the potatoes.

This was a big hit at my house. I blanched some fresh peas and threw them into the mix at the end. A pretty dish and tasty. Perfect for summer as the vegetables start to come in. Next week I could throw on some corn.

comments.recipe_name: roasted broccoli with tahini garlic sauce
comments.comment: The tahini sauce is a winner and could be used with so many things. I was lucky to have tahini from seed+mill, the halva store in the Chelsea Market, and this sauce was divine. I liked the roasted broccoli but my husband didn't get how broccoli could be crunchy. I thought it quite wonderful!

This was very easy and quick to make. I shredded the cabbage in the food processor and used a supermarket cider (the quality shouldn't matter as it's being boiled down). I quite liked the variety of seeds and used the celery seeds plus a combination of yellow and brown mustard seeds. I did not feel that they added a bitterness and do not see the need to fry them.

comments.recipe_name: green beans corn and carrot salad
comments.comment: Nice combination of vegetables: looks and tastes good. I agree that too much dressing was called for in the recipe. Use what you think works and save the rest for another salad. The proportions in the dressing are good.

A great way to use herbs (the more you cut the more they grow). Looks great, tastes delicious. Lovely on a summer's evening with a chicken dish, perhaps. I didn't use the soy sauce this time. With the cilantro crop disappearing in the heat, I think I'll try it with basil next time.

Wonderful. Easy to make. I too used a springform pan lined with parchment (after reading all the comments) but I imagine it would be just as good with a regular 9" pan. You might just have to bring the pan to the table. I always use berries that are dry and I don't squish them when folding or when spreading the batter in the pan.

Superb pancakes. No problem cutting the recipe in half. Just don't over-stir the batter. The batter was quite thick but pourable. Pancakes turned out unusually light and flavorful. So easy and so perfect when blueberries are in season.

This is a great recipe. Making a ratatouille on a baking sheet in brilliant (as opposed to cooking vegetables separately in pans or cooking them together and having them mushy). The recipe is very versatile. I loved the addition of fennel. Wonderful with vegetables from the farmstand but also good as Mark says, with supermarket vegetables.
Lovely dish. I used it as a side to a roast chicken and pasta was tasty and visually appealing. Next time I might spice it up a bit but then again, it was like summer comfort food.

This cake was easy to make, not too sweet, and delicious the next 2 mornings with my coffee. The crumb is especially nice. Set timer for 50 minutes to start testing for doneness; you want the cake light and moist.

comments.recipe_name: chickpea quinoa and celery salad with middle eastern flavors
comments.comment: I enjoyed the textures of this salad. Easy to put together, versatile, and keeps. Tastes like summer.

What to do with a quarter pound ground beef and a quarter pound ground venison? It was delicious. I made enough spice mix, onions, etc., for the full recipe and served it with the rice. The 1/2 combined made about 15 good-sized meatballs so if you are working with a full pound I recommend you do it in batches. The meatballs were very tasty. I didn't have a poblano chile but I did not half a jalapeno and it started to pour as I went to pick the mint, so no mint. A nice dinner for 2.

comments.recipe_name: red bean salad with walnuts and fresh herbs
comments.comment: Refreshing, keeps well, easy to prepare, complex flavor. I used canned kidney beans. And attractive!

comments.recipe_name: pan cooked summer squash with tomatoes and basil
comments.comment: What could be more pleasant than picking zucchini and tomatoes from your garden (or purchasing from a farmers' market) and putting this together quickly? The kitchen smells like summer. I served it lukewarm over thin spaghetti. Ah, summer!
More an inspiration than a recipe, this is the perfect way to serve perfect tomatoes. They must be good tomatoes; nothing else matters.

Delicious. Mince your garlic quite fine so there aren't big chunks and try to leave as much of the garlic in the frying pan when you remove the shrimp. You won't find it distasteful. I used an Albarino and it was delicious. I thought the addition of the lemon juice made all the difference in the world and I used torn basil in lieu of parsley. The key as usual is to have very good shrimp.

Very nice. My husband didn't want me to add the yogurt (he did a major tasting before I served the dish) but I thought the yogurt really elevated the dish. It really did taste Turkish! I used a variegated oregano instead of the mint, put it on top of the yogurt, and thought it was delicious.
Very nice summer side dish. I did not use cheese and did not miss it. I served it just above room temperature.

comments.recipe_name: tomato fresh fig and blue cheese salad
comments.comment: I picked the figs, I picked the tomatoes, I made this salad. Used a supermarket blue cheese. This is August on a plate.

comments.recipe_name: spiced basmati rice and sweet corn pilaf
comments.comment: Very nice flavors. My only problem with the dish is that the peppercorns, cloves, and cardamon are left whole when you serve it. Should they come out or be squashed at at certain point? I served this dish as a side to a rack of lamb and did not make the raita. I liked the lightness of the rice and corn.
Wonderful dish. Great way to use some bruised tomatoes. Grating is just so easy and so little waste. Ah, summer!

delicious and fast. Roasted eggplant on the gas grill. didn't have jar of capers open so just added more salt. Good idea found below to use lemon juice in lieu of vinegar (for next time). Leave time for this to cool. Mine was more like a dip than the photo shows but very good.

I cut 3 very large honeycrisp apple into 16 pieces each and then very quickly assembled this cake. It turned out beautifully, the apples on top intact and the cake moist. I folded into the batter about 8 slices and used the rest to make "closely fitting" concentric circles on top. I brushed the apples with the Calvados liquid (I had put at least 2 TBSP in with the apples) and sprinkled sugar on top. I baked at 375 for 65 minutes (thank you other bakers for that tip). Perfect!

This was delicious. I served it as a side (no marinara) and was pleased with the flavors. I only had quick-cooking polenta so I don't know if that is why it was clumpy but it didn't really matter. I'll make it again.

comments.recipe_name: stir fried bok choy or sturdy greens
comments.comment: Very nice side dish. I think that next time I would just pour boiling water over the greens to blanch rather than dirty another pot.

The 20 minutes was the perfect amount of cooking time: the fish was done and the clams had just opened. I quite liked this recipe, a complete dinner in a bowl. The flavors were subtle (if anything, I would not use the hot pepper). I used a good fish broth from my fishmonger (that may be why it came together so well) and I thought my saffron wasn't pungent enough so I added some of La Boite's Salvador spice mix. Based on the previous comment, I removed the lemon slices before serving.

Excellent. I too used the second half of the butter to quickly saute spinach. The skin that had stuck to the pan quickly came up and was delicious. I served the fish over the greens. Picture perfect. (And I too did not add any more salt. The lemon cut the saltiness of the anchovies and that was fine.) Cooking time for my thick fillets was 12 minutes--go figure.

Lovely dish. I served it over polenta as suggested. My only comment is that the white meat was slightly overcooked when the dark was finished. Sauce was delicious and I would definately make this again. I would only use the oven to keep the dish warm. I used large black unpitted olives and they added a sparkle to the dish. Loved it.

comments.recipe_name: pasta with cauliflower spicy tomato sauce and capers
comments.comment: A nice quick dinner,, just don't overcook the cauliflower. I used thin spaghetti and pecorino. The cheese adds a lot of flavor to this very neutral dish.

comments.recipe_name: hearty quinoa and white bean soup
comments.comment: Delicious. Make it your own. Quinoa adds to appearance and density. Lasts for a few days if not eaten first. I used a can of white kidney beans, half chicken and vegetable stock, and the vegetables I had in the refrigerator at that moment, and rosemary and thyme. Flavors are very nice.
Delicious. However, didn't need to roast 20 minutes in the oven despite an initial rest of about 2 hours. So use your meat thermometer if you like your lamb rare or medium rare.
A nice variation for a side dish. And I really liked that you can do most of it ahead of time and then pop into the oven to finish.

comments.recipe_name: lemony pasta with chickpeas and parsley
comments.comment: I liked this as a side dish to a roast chicken. It really is a comfort food, relatively bland but with a great textural interest and a enjoyable mellowness. I might add more garlic and/or more lemon to it, maybe some tarragon, but I quite liked the combination of the pasta and cick peas.

comments.recipe_name: sheet pan coriander chicken with caramelized brussels sprouts
comments.comment: Really, really good. Chicken was very moist (used a small, local chicken) and brussels sprouts were delicious. This is a great dish and easy dish. I marinated the chicken and brussels sprout in a plastic bag for 8 hours in the refrigerator and then took them out for an hour to get close to room temperature before putting in the oven. Flavor was wonderful, no bitterness at all, and just really juicy.

Awesome soup (and it is a soup)! I cut the meat into bite-size pieces and basically followed the instructions (using low-salt chicken stock) and cooked through step 5. The soup at that point was cooled and refrigerated. The flavors just came together overnight. I added the greens and lemon and this was delicious before serving. I particularly loved the turnips. A wonderful soup, a great meal; make it your own!
Perfect roast chicken. Buy a good bird!!! Have it out of the refrigerator a bit prior to roasting. And use an instant-read thermometer to make sure it's reached 165.

comments.recipe_name: sheet pan chicken with potatoes arugula and garlic yogurt
comments.comment: Delicious. For 2 of us I used 2 thighs and 4 drumsticks. Marinated in a bag for 6 hours. I thought I would regret using that much harissa but no, it was savory not hot. The potatoes and chicken cooked perfectly in 40 minutes, the leek mixture for the 25 minutes. I put the yogurt on top (I spent much too much time looking for non-Greek yogurt and don't know if what I ended up with was so-called Greek style. Why does it matter in this recipe?) Worked fine. Arugula, no dill. Attractive plate.

comments.recipe_name: caramelized turnips with capers lemon and parsley
comments.comment: A nice change. I've never sauteed turnips before. The lemon and capers complemented the grilled fish I served this with. And I liked that I could make it in advance and then reheat.
Delicious!. Complex and so different from the regular Ashkenazi charoset. This recipe makes a lot. Even though I served 12 people and they ate quite a bit, we didn't use half.

comments.recipe_name: roasted carrot salad with arugula and pomegranate
comments.comment: Absolutely wonderful and refreshing salad. I was lucky to have sweet carrots and fresh arugula. Loved the contrasts in color, flavors, textures. Keep it light. I did not add the walnuts or yogurt. This was a delicious salad!
Delicious dish. Love the colors, textures, flavors, and ease of assembly.

Fabulous! I made the cake 2 days prior to serving, the caramelized walnuts, the day before, and the whipped cream shortly before serving. I used dark brown sugar and can't imagine it suffered terribly for that (I will look for muscovado for next time). Otherwise, I followed the recipe and found it to be easy and the result wonderful. The nuts are crunchy, the cake moist, the cream luscious. Not just for Passover!
This method works well for fillets. Put cast iron pan directly into oven after making mushrooms to keep warm.

comments.recipe_name: crab pasta with snap peas and mint
comments.comment: This is a nice dish. I suggest adding garlic to the mix and using basil in lieu of mint. I liked the creaminess and the lemon but felt it needed more flavor (that's where the garlic comes in) to make the crab shine.

Wonderful side dish. I used thin spaghetti, mixed mushrooms from the farmers' market, and an herb (maybe marjoram) growing outside that the groundhog did not eat (he finished off the parsley). Really lovely aroma and delicious taste. I liked it both with and without the cheese. As a side or course before the main course, use about 2-2.5 oz per person and .5 lb of mushrooms.

Delicious! While I've made many chickens with an herb-butter mixture under the skin, I have never done it with just the herbs. It still came out moist and even more flavorful. The chicken was at dry and at room temperature when put into the oven surrounded by root vegetables. I did add some water after the first 20 minutes. To make the gravy I added some wine instead of the chicken stock and smushed (and then removed) the lemon. Wonderful flavor.

An excuse to harvest my sorrel! This dish was easy and delicious. Scallops tender and sweet and a great foil for lemony sorrel. I like the idea of browning on one side and then finishing in the sauce, It insures that the scallops aren't overcooked.

comments.recipe_name: pasta fredda with cherry tomatoes anchovies and herbs
comments.comment: Wonderful summer dish. Very variable depending on what pasta and herbs you have on hand. I love that the tomatoes aren't cooked and how it all seems to blend together so well.

Delicious. I too added a splash of white wine and served over 2 slices of a baguette in a soup dish. Very easy way to cook clams. Next time I'll use lemon instead of lime with the chives and tarragon.

comments.recipe_name: vietnamese grilled duck salad with cucumber radishes and peanuts
comments.comment: What to do with leftover roast duck! Excellent summer supper. I went right to step 2 and made the salad and vinaigrette, Served all on a bed of lettuce. Tasty and you can add whatever vegetables you have on hand.

comments.recipe_name: pasta with tomatoes capers olives and breadcrumbs
comments.comment: Very nice. I used day-old bread for the bread crumbs, which I crumbled and then put in the toaster oven for a few minutes before sauteing with the garlic. I did not discard the garlic. I thought the sauce was a bit sharp but when put over the pasta it was just right. I too added some pasta water (maybe 1/2 cup) and a spritz of olive oil to finish.

comments.recipe_name: pasta with caramelized onion swiss chard and garlicky bread crumbs
comments.comment: Using fresh Swiss chard and regular spaghetti (neither fusilli nor whole wheat) this was quite a delicious side dish. I finished it with about a couple of TBSPs of butter. I also added about a cup of fresh peas after adding the chard. Complex flavors and textures. Very nice.
Nice dinner. I used a small cut-up chicken. Very easy to prepare and very tasty. chicken stuck to pan a bit so next time I would butter pan thoroughly.

comments.recipe_name: yotam ottolenghis chermoula eggplant with bulgur and yogurt
comments.comment: This was good but not the best. Too many elements left me wondering what I was eating and why so many disparate ingredients were in the dish (which I served as a side). My bulgur must not have been "fine," as it needed much more time and water to soften but that was okay. Just keep tasting until the texture is right.

Even though I managed to overcook both the pasta (trofie) and broccoli, this turned out delicious. The grated cheese added a creaminess. The broccoli flavor is pronounced and the dish has a great aroma (if you like broccoli).

10 stars. This is what Sicilian cooking tastes like now (I was recently there)! I used fresh sardines and I can't imagine making this with canned. The flavors burst out: the sweet of the raisins, the salt of the anchovies, the mild licorice of the fennel. as someone else remarked, there are so many layers to this dish. Don't be put off by the long list of ingredients. It comes together quickly and the reward is great.

comments.recipe_name: quinoa pilaf with sweet peas and green garlic
comments.comment: Very nice when fresh peas are in season. Perhaps try basil instead of the tarragon. It needed a little oomph.

Lovely summer dessert. I used fresh blueberries (New Jersey, of course). Looked good although not as puffy as some clafoutis, tasted great, and was very light, perfect on a hot evening. Next time I would sprinkle a little confectioner's sugar on top and/or serve with vanilla ice cream. Leftover slice warmed up nicely (20 sec.) in microwave the next morning.

Easy and a great way to use extra corn. I made it with cherry tomatoes (not a fan of cooked cucumber) and mixed it with a handful of greens before serving. The dish is attractive, seasonal, and healthy. I served it with a duck breast.

A great way to serve up extra corn. I used basil instead of mint; tarragon would be good too. Also might be nice to add some green or red pepper to the corn. This is a great casual company dish. So may people find corn on the cob difficult, this way the cook is doing the work for them and, you'll see, they'll scoop this up.

What a delicious fast lunch! Basically following the instructions and list of ingredients I stopped at less than 1/2 pound of fresh kale and that was plenty. Soup was rich, very flavorful, and filling.

Lovely side dish. The fresh corn adds a sensational sweetness to the pasta. I served it with salmon and didn't want it to reach "gooey," but it was close. Also, I used a small red onion instead of the scallions and plain old water instead of the broth. Easy and tasty.

comments.recipe_name: pasta with anchovies garlic chiles and kale
comments.comment: An excellent way to use kale. This dish was quite delicious and easy to assemble. I think it needs that squeeze of lemon (to offset the saltiness). I had no problem with the pasta getting mushy; just cook it as the recipe says to al dente and then drain. It will reheat with the kale.

Delicious! I basically followed the recipe and was surprised that there was enough sauce. I did use good Italian pasta (boy, that makes a difference). Layering it as suggested and using the ricotta salata made the dish creamy and sumptuous. This really is a great recipe. The sauce can be made ahead of time and rewarmed. Having been to Sicily recently, where this dish is a staple on the menu, I can say that this is a good adaptation. It's simple and makes use of seasonal ingredients.

comments.recipe_name: roast chicken salad with croutons and shallot dressing
comments.comment: Delicious salad. I had half of a very juicy chicken, a few slices of sourdough bread for croutons, and some beautiful greens, and these ingredients came together to make a delicious entree salad. In Step 3, I shredded the chicken and put it and the juices into a frying pan for a couple of minutes so that the juices had melted and the chicken was at room temperature. It wasn't warm enough to wilt the lettuce. I tossed it all together in a large bowl and topped with the thin slices of cheese.

comments.recipe_name: greek style fish with marinated tomatoes
comments.comment: We grilled a 3-lb blue fish. Absolutely delicious! I loved the marinated cherry tomatoes with it. The flavors just rang out. We make a lot of whole fish and when cherry tomatoes are in season I imagine they will grace many more fish from now on in my house.

Very enjoyable dish. Mellow and indulgent. In another note, someone mentioned "overcooked" broccoli. The broccoli isn't meant to remain crisp. It adds color and flavor, but becomes a part of the dish. I suggest cutting your florets and stalks into very small pieces. I added them all at the same time and it was fine.
Excellent salad. I served it as a main course for vegetarian lunch. It was a big hit. I liked the different textures and interesting flavors. I used a cut-up shallot instead of the scallions.

comments.recipe_name: garlic and thyme roasted chicken with crispy drippings croutons
comments.comment: Wow! This was good. I used a slightly under 3 lb. chicken from D'Artagnan and a few left-over slices of homemade bread. Almost sinful. Cooked in a Staub pan, which is dark but didn't burn the bread. Chicken was done in about 45 minutes. Herbs and garlic permeated the chicken. Garlic wasn't done in that time so couldn't be used to spread on bread but it was still delicious.

Delicious , attractive, and simple. I served it over trofie pasta (little tolled thingies) with plenty of pepper and it was yummy. I made it about an hour before serving and kept warm on stove. Colors were still bright and vegetables not overcooked.

I adore this! Used a cut-up Griggstown chicken (under 3 pounds) and followed the recipe. It does cook quickly if you have browned it properly. I did not pour off the fat. I left it on the stove after it was just done and reheated a couple of hours later and served over pasta. Absolutely delicious! The sauce is wonderful.

comments.recipe_name: spicy couscous salad with tomatoes green beans and peppers
comments.comment: I used 1/2 a pound of Israeli couscous for this amount of vegetables. Very tasty. It does take time to prep and cook. This is a wonderful dish for using up the CSA basket. A little more of this and a little less of that will work fine. After assembling I put the salad into the refrigerator and then tossed before serving.

comments.recipe_name: black and arborio risotto with beets and beet greens
comments.comment: This was good, not great. I like to use the beet greens so I tried it and found the recipe took more time than it was worth. I really love beets and thought they were lost in this "concoction."

comments.recipe_name: smoked whitefish salad with creme fraiche and capers
comments.comment: I used Greek yogurt (0% fat) with a little mayonnaise. My fish was very salty so my product was salty but the texture and flavor were quite nice. Maybe I'll add some finely chopped celery today to it and see if that lightens the saltiness.

I asked my fish purveyor for a good substitute for the striped bass and went home with an "underutilized" fish called wild tail, I believe, from Florida. Perfect. I had no problem with the potatoes as I diced them very small and cooked them on the lowest flame until we finished appetizers. The fish was done quickly and was so tender. I added the fennel fronds to my Thai basil for the garnish and played around with spices until I reached the flavor I wanted. Delicious and easy dish.

comments.recipe_name: lasagna with spinach and roasted zucchini
comments.comment: A nice meatless dinner! I used a 9x11 inch pan. Even though I used Rao's garlic marinara I felt this needed a little kick. I would add some crushed red pepper and omit the cinnamon, or maybe some more assertive herbs. Also it could have used another few ounces of the ricotta mixture. The spinach zucchini mix was nice, colorful, and tasty.

comments.recipe_name: chicken tagine with eggplant and olives
comments.comment: So good that I'd serve this to company! The spice mix is excellent (cumin plus smoked paprika) and you should make enough to coat the chicken. I was surprised that the eggplant softened and sweetened up as much as it did by steaming. Served over couscous and with a salad, this makes for an excellent dinner. Oh, and I used a fancy-pants tagine and it worked just fine although it is heavy to carry over to the table. Ah, but the aromas when you lift the lid!

I returned to this recipe after working with with sourdough for a few months and made an" ideal" loaf. You really need to work with sourdough to understand its properties. A big change: I use a proofing basket (Amazon) for my second rise (with cornmeal.)I can deflate and fold in the basket. I turn the basket into the hot pot and make 2 large gashes on top. The bread rose more than the one in the photo; the flavor was perfect; and me and my starter are quite pleased with ourselves.

comments.recipe_name: halibut with brown butter lemon and sage
comments.comment: Superb! Used my non-stick pan and followed directions. Yes, halibut is expensive but fresh halibut is a fantastic fish. This was simple yet very sophisticated on the plate. I think 6 oz per person is enough as long as you have some sides and a salad.

Delicious cake. Pronounced lemon flavor that mellows as the hours pass. Very easy to make, it's light (not dense), and attractive (I used the marmalade glaze). And instead of whisking by hand, I used the whisk attachment in my mixer; perhaps that's why it was so light. I took it out at 70 minutes--perfect.

comments.recipe_name: risotto with spring carrots and leeks
comments.comment: Very pretty and very good. The lemon at the end brightened the flavor up so I recommend using it. I only needed about 5 1/2 cups of broth to reach al dente.
Easy dish. Looks good, tastes good, compliments almost anything. Use whatever vegetables and herbs you have available.

I agree with the majority here: this is a good dish but not the knock-out I expected. I liked the layered aspect of it, the bean "binder" is a wonderful idea, being able to make this in advance is a great plus; but somehow it didn't add up to the sum of its parts. It's possible that all it needed was more garlic and some cayenne.

comments.recipe_name: sourdough stuffing with kale and dates
comments.comment: Excellent. I loved the variety of textures and flavors. I make my own sourdough bread so I was thrilled to find this recipe. I did not have the chiles so I added some red pepper flakes. Also, the only kind of date I could find in my supermarket was medjoul so I bought those, only to realize when I got home that I had a package of Sun-maid chopped dates at home. The Sun-maid package says they are deglet noor. You learn something new every day! I think I'll make this with roast chicken too.

Well I sorta of changed the recipe by using venison "cutlets," which didn't taste like chicken but we might have mistaken for veal. It was very good. If making again, and I might if I find more venison cutlets hidden in my freezer, I would thin out the gravy somewhat. The mustard-vinegar was a wonderful complement to the dish as were the mushrooms and I wanted more of it. In the marinade, I skipped the thyme and used crushed juniper berries.

comments.recipe_name: roasted beet salad with goat cheese and walnuts
comments.comment: Beets, walnuts, and goat cheese remain a delicious combination even if it's become a cliche. This is simple and beautiful. The beets can be roasted and cut in advance. A lovely first course to any dinner.

Chicken was delicious! I used what I had: chicken drumsticks, a mix of regular and sweet potatoes, and I braised cabbage separately. A wonderful, complete dinner. The sauce was made from 0-fat Greek yogurt and breading was panko.

Wonderful way to serve beets. I usually roast them or pickle them and this is a nice change. Use stock, water, whatever to cover. I cooked them a few hours in advance and then warmed them up just before serving. And I used older beets, ones I thought maybe were too old, but they were just right for this recipe, just a little more difficult to peel (as they were soft).

This is delicious. I was using it as a side dish for 2 people so I basically cut the recipe in half, using about 7/8s of a cup of stone-ground yellow corn meal to make the polenta. Next time I would use no more than 1/2 cup of cornmeal but use plenty of mushrooms, at least 6 oz.

comments.recipe_name: pan seared salmon with celery olives and capers
comments.comment: This is what Sicily tastes like. Based on readers' suggestions I did not add the caper brine and I didn't have the full complement of currants (55 g instead of 100). Flavors were so good. As there were just the two of us, I made the full amount of sauce, which I also served over pasta (a small spiral called strozzapreti) and added a tablespoon or two of last summer's pesto Not bad at all! I loved the textures and sweet, salty, sour balance.

comments.recipe_name: stuffed roasted yellow peppers or red peppers in tomato sauce
comments.comment: This serves 4 as a side dish. What I did for the 2 of us: I made 2 peppers (they turned out great) and realizing this wasn't anywhere close to enough for dinner I made a bunch of small and light meatballs. The peppers in the marinara were surrounded by these little meatballs. Looked nice and tasted delicious. With a salad and bread, it made for a nice winter dinner. Also, I was able to clean the peppers without making the slits.
Not my favorite bread but it works, it's easy and tasty. The flavor was excellent and it was nice and crusty. Almost cakelike. Lightly toast the next morning and serve with bread and jam.

A great dish to feed a crowd. It comes together quickly. It's light; colorful, and easy though the prep takes some time. I shredded the cabbage in my food processor. Make ahead of time, keep warm, and fluff before serving.

comments.recipe_name: slow roasted citrus salmon with herb salad
comments.comment: Very attractive presentation. I prepared it ahead and then roasted about 45 minutes before serving. The citrus brought out the sweetness of the salmon. I think this is an excellent "company dish."

A big hit. My clementines (or were they mandarins?) were large so the gram measurement really helped. I glazed it with the chocolate the day after baking (using the old trick of putting pieces of waxed paper under the cake and pulling them out after glaze had semi- dried). My clementine slices basically disintegrated but I used them anyway. The cake took on a modernist look and was as delicious as it was stylish.

Fantastic! Easy, peasy. Probably 6 oz of chocolate would have been sufficient so the extra was a baker's treat. I used it on the clementine cake and hope I can remember to use it on many other cakes. As always, it's only as good as your ingredients so use a good chocolate.

Delicious! As a roast chicken fan (I'll roast it most any way as long as it turns out moist and and the skin crispy), now I know what to do with half a carton of buttermilk. The finished chicken looked different than any chicken I've ever made--the skin turns a dark brown and it's flat--but it met all my criteria for a delicious chicken.

comments.recipe_name: wild rice almond and mushroom stuffing
comments.comment: I used half wild rice and half black rice. While it tasted wonderful, the black rice obscured the wild rice and I considered it a waste of (expensive) wild rice. Next time I'll stick with the wild rice or try wild with white rice. I served it as a side to a roast chicken and the dish was delicious and it's always a plus when it can be finished ahead of time and kept warm.

comments.recipe_name: garlicky chicken thighs with scallion and lime
comments.comment: Absolutely delicious way to cook chicken thighs, my favorite part of the chicken. I browned the chicken and then let it sit for close to an hour before adding the rest of the ingredients and simmering.. The sauce was excellent--I too used chicken broth instead of water, because I had some open--and the garlic just wonderful smooshed on some bread or just sucked out. Chicken was very tasty.

Delicious side dish when asparagus are plentiful. I used about a half of a small jalapeno for 7 oz of asparagus. I stir-fried for a couple of minutes, not just one, before adding garlic, etc. So easy and it comes together so quickly.

A very nice addition to my asparagus repertoire. I parboiled the asparagus and made the salsa a few hours in advance. Dish was pretty (I think I too would use feta next time instead of a soft chevre) and the salsa, without the cheese, went well with the salmon I served with the asparagus.

comments.recipe_name: pasta with foie gras and truffles
comments.comment: Wow. If black truffles (brought home from Croatia) ever come your way, this a a wonderful way to use them. For 4 people as an appetizer, about 6 oz of pappardelle, the truffles (about 1/2 oz I think; we sliced them with a mandoline), and a cylinder of D'artagnan foie gras. I should have brought home more truffles. This was my first time using truffles at home and I can now envision so many ways to use them.

comments.recipe_name: braised chicken with artichokes and olives
comments.comment: A delicious dinner, served over orzo, similar to a chicken cacciatore. I used chicken parts (thighs and legs from D'artagnan) and frozen artichokes. The chicken was fall-off-the-bone tender (I had kept it warm in the turned-off oven for about an hour). I did not put it under the broiler; I did grate Parmesan over it before serving and added some lemon juice. This could be made a day in advance and warmed up.

comments.recipe_name: flattened chicken thighs with roasted lemon slices
comments.comment: Flavorful and tender. I loved the crispy skin. The best way to check if chicken is done is with a thermometer. The 20 minutes in the oven was enough. Especially delicious when herbs are from your own garden. I used the parchment paper between the two cast-iron pans and that worked well.

This was excellent with lamb. After step #2, I suggest draining the couscous well (if water is still present) and putting it into a salad bowl. Then add all the other ingredients. On the other hand you could have the other ingredients in a bowl awaiting the couscous. Then the bowl can be put into the refrigerator until dinner is ready.
When everything is fresh and local, this is fantastic. I used the Aleppo pepper and a Bulgarian feta (Krinos White Cheese). Wonderful on a summer night as a side to a grilled fish.
Absolutely delicious. I added minced garlic and rosemary to the olive oil and let sit while grill got hot. Served over arugula. So good!

Easy and delicious. I used a 3-lb. package of local chicken drumsticks. Served with a salad and quinoa with corn and zucchini. Nice summer supper. I loved the mustard mix and then the panko crisp. I made plenty of the mustard-butter mix and put a little under the skin of the legs.

comments.recipe_name: simple lamb curry with carrot raita
comments.comment: I too just made the carrot raita, which was delicious as a condiment for grilled lamb chops. When the weather is colder, I'll make the lamb curry.

comments.recipe_name: corn pudding with roasted garlic and sage
comments.comment: A great dish when fresh corn is plentiful. I served a lot of food when we had company over and this was devoured. What I most liked about it was that I put it together hours ahead, and then stirred and poured it into the baking/serving dish and put it into the oven about 75 minutes before the entree was to be served, just as the recipe suggests. It was perfect. Corn this year is super sweet so even though I thought I had added a lot of pepper, to my taste it needed a lot more.

comments.recipe_name: greek stewed green beans and yellow squash with tomatoes
comments.comment: Wonderful with fresh vegetables! The vegetables were so sweet even with the lemon added. What I like most is that this dish can be made ahead of time and allowed to sit (it will just develop more) and then served at room temperature.

This dish was fun, pretty, and also delicious. I had a bunch of young muti-colored beets and cooked them as directed. Before serving, and still warm, I split them and dressed them like a baked potato with butter, a dab of sour cream, and chives. They looked great and added some festivity to the meal. The skin peeled right off as we ate them. I think I would peel them next time prior to splitting.

comments.recipe_name: pan seared steak with red wine sauce
comments.comment: I made the sauce to accompany a grilled steak and it was excellent. I thinly sliced the shallots and added them to melted butter in a small saucepan. After browning I added a bit of cognac and did not fire it up. It evaporated on its own in an instant. The wine and chicken stock also almost completely evaporated. Kept warm until just before serving, then whisked in the butter and chives. Delicious.

Nice dish. I used chicken stock and, as many others reported, only needed about 3 1/2-4 cups of it. I used a shallot instead of the leek and added a diced green pepper with the corn. And I omitted the cream but I did use the chives. Mild in flavor, filling, and attractive.

comments.recipe_name: roast chicken with maple butter and rosemary
comments.comment: Five stars for appearance but only four for flavor. My husband discerned the sweetness (and disliked it) right off. I would make this chicken again but I would be very generous with black pepper in the glaze. I cooked it over broccoli, small potatoes, and large-diced sweet potatoes. I started the vegetables, dotted with butter, in the oven for 15 minutes prior to putting a rack on top of the pan and placing the chicken on top of it. Veggies were great. Chicken was very juicy and took 45 minutes

comments.recipe_name: chickpea vegetable soup with parmesan rosemary and lemon
comments.comment: Basically followed the recipe but used 4 cups chicken broth and 1 cup water. Very pretty in the soup bowl; the Parmesan-lemon sprinkle adds a depth of flavor; lovely dinner with a salad and a loaf of fresh bread. I removed the clove from the onion, the rosemary, rosemary sprig, the bay leaf, and the Parmesan rind before serving. I never add much salt to my food so I added it to taste at the end of cooking (both the low-sodium chicken broth and Parmesan have intrinsic salt and celery? does too).

comments.recipe_name: shredded red cabbage and carrot salad
comments.comment: Attractive and refreshing. I didn't have the dill and while I'm sure it adds that zesty dilly flavor that only dill can impart, the salad was still excellent. And can be made ahead, always a plus!

comments.recipe_name: roasted cauliflower gratin with tomatoes and goat cheese
comments.comment: Others at our Thanksgiving feast enjoyed this dish more than I did. I loved that I could make it a day in advance and that it travels well. But I didn't like the appearance of it on the plate and something about the taste--I think it was the cinnamon--didn't work well for me. I would make it again as I liked the roasted cauliflower and cheese idea (I used 4 oz of a soft goat cheese) but I'd go with a more herbal seasoning, maybe oregano.

comments.recipe_name: italian style braised rabbit with rosemary and mushrooms
comments.comment: Fantastic. Beautiful, relatively easy to make, and can be made in advance. First time I haven't removed kidneys on a rabbit and we don't know what happened to them, i.e., if we ate them (and we must have) we were not aware. Anyway, I used 2 large leeks, 12 oz of fresh mushrooms, the mushroom water in lieu of the chicken broth, and 2-3 dozen cherry tomatoes instead of canned. Covered dish with parchment and then lid before braising. Served over pappardelle.

Delicious. I used a D'Artagnan rabbit. I needed to go out after I planned to put the rabbit in the oven so I turned the heat down to 300 (250 might have even been better)and covered the pan with parchment before putting on the lid (completely covered) and returned home 4 hours later to find the most delectable dish (what an aroma). I mixed the mustard with about another half cup of chicken stock to refresh and served over egg noodles.

This wonderful dessert is basically too sweet for me (and I decreased the sugar a bit). But I want to add something to the discussion anyways. I enjoyed the cake more the second day. The pieces, i.e., the pavlova, the mousse, the creme fraiche, and the strawberries, seemed more of one piece after a night in the refrigerator. And it cut better too. I had some difficulty stirring the whipped cream into the chocolate in step 8. I did it but it took work. Anyone else have that problem?

comments.recipe_name: roasted butternut squash and red onions
comments.comment: Delicious and very attractive. I used the wonderful Seed + Mill tahini and it's just a great product, much better than the supermarket tahini I've been using for years. The sauce drizzled on and looked so pretty along with pine nuts and parsley over the roasted butternut squash and red onions.

comments.recipe_name: garlic braised short ribs with red wine
comments.comment: Delicious. For the two of us I used 1.3 lbs of bison short ribs (from the Readington River Buffalo Farm in Flemington NJ) and basically cut the rest of the recipe in half. Bison has virtually no fat so need need to separate the fat from the sauce. One trick I use when braising is covering the food with parchment paper, under the lid, prior to putting in the oven. It ensures the meat will not dry out at all.

comments.recipe_name: roasted halibut and potatoes with rosemary
comments.comment: The halibut was very moist and the flavor clean and bright. Delicious preparation.

comments.recipe_name: italian wedding soup with turkey meatballs
comments.comment: This is a perfectly good soup, not a great one, but certainly the best Italian wedding soup I've ever had (they are always salted beyond redemption in my experience). I made small meatballs--I think the pound of ground turkey made 24. The soup was fine the next day too topped with more parmesan and black pepper. It's a pleasant variation for chicken soup.

comments.recipe_name: seared scallop pasta with burst tomatoes and herbs
comments.comment: Beautiful and delicious. For the two of us I cooked 12 oz of pasta and about 12 oz of scallops (7 beautiful scallops from Metropolitan Seafood in Lebanon NJ). I sauteed a small yellow onion instead of the shallots and my greens were a combo of parsley, chives, and spinach. With a green salad this was too much for two relatively big eaters. This dish would be an excellent first course pasta, 2 oz of pasta with a large scallop on top. It's a very rich dish and very delicious.

comments.recipe_name: baby bok choy with oyster sauce
comments.comment: Great and easy way to cook baby bok choy. Used it as a side to a beef stir fry. I omitted the sugar--used a little mirin instead. Next time I would cut the vegetable in half for smaller pieces. FYI: the supermarket brand of oyster sauce I used--Kame--does not list MSG as an ingredient.

comments.recipe_name: stir fried beef and sugar snap peas
comments.comment: Used this as a base for a no-recipe day because we had a good-sized piece of a rib-eye steak left over. I bought the sugar snap peas and and also bean sprouts but besides those 2 things I had everything in the pantry. I skipped step one. Made a sauce from mushroom soaking liquid (I added soaked dried mushrooms), mirin, tamari, rice vinegar, and cornstarch. Stir fried pretty much as directed but also added water chestnuts and baby corn. Served over rice with baby bok choy side. No complaints.

Recipe made 25 good-sized cookies. I think I would make them even smaller next time. Used bittersweet chocolate chips and loved the flavors. Cookies are very soft when taken out of the oven (at 16-18 minutes) so don't try to take them off the cookie sheet until they have cooled a bit. I used an ice cream scoop to form the cookies and rounded scoops into little balls.

comments.recipe_name: pressure cooker corned beef and cabbage
comments.comment: Excellent recipe. I used Sauvignon blanc for the wine, added white turnips to the vegetables, and otherwise followed the instructions using a 2-lb low-salt corned beef. And I skipped the honey. I should have spread the mustard on the non-fatty surface but didn't think of that in time. We devoured this. The vegetables were perfect, the meat melted, and the dish, for the first time, wasn't too salty or sour. Just delicious.Perhaps this once-a-year dish should be repeated before next St. Pat's day!

comments.recipe_name: farro and arborio risotto with leeks herbs and lemon
comments.comment: Wonderful dish. I halved it for a side dish for two and we devoured all of it. I used Carnaroli rice, farro, and parsley. I loved the appearance, the texture, and the taste. Would be wonderful with mushrooms!

Refreshing, attractive salad. I made it ahead to see if the sliced Granny Smith apples would brown and they didn't. I sliced a knife and my slices were fine. I'm thinking of serving this for Passover along side the heavy entrees.

This is a wonderful addition to Passover cooking. "Matsagna" was new to me. Vegetarians will love it. And I love another main course alternative for the week. Anyway, I followed the recipe faithfully and it worked perfectly. I needed 1 1/2 pieces of matza per layer (matza generally comes in a 16-oz box). The recipe completely filled my standard 9x13 pan and din't run over although I was afraid it would. I assembled it a day ahead and baked it before serving. The pieces stayed together when cut.

Superb! First of all I had no trouble with the garlic burning. I minced it finely and when it came to searing had no problem. After the long cooking I refrigerated the brisket and the next day skimmed the fat off the top and sliced the meat thinly before reheating. I served the salad in a bowl alongside the meat and there were no leftovers.

I made this with a roast of venison. The flavor was wonderful. Made the day before serving, sliced the next day, and reheated in the oven. As there is next to no fat in venison you need to make sure the meat stays moist. Very nice.

comments.recipe_name: marsala marinated chicken with roasted vegetables
comments.comment: Delicious. I love sheet-pan dinners! Chicken skin was crisp and meat was tender. Flavors wonderful. Vegetables wonderful. The marinade could have been scaled down a bit but you do want the chicken to be in enough liquid (I refrigerated about 5 hours). The salad was nice. I added quite a bit more mustard to the salad dressing. The chicken was finished about an hour before serving. I covered it with foil, added the vegetables when they were done, and kept warm until serving. Excellent.

comments.recipe_name: pan fried halibut with spiced chickpea and herb salad
comments.comment: This was delicious but I must agree that it is a bit too complicated for a quick dinner. Too many bowls. The finished product was fantastic, fine-restaurant appeal, and very flavorful. Next time I would make both the chick peas and salad ahead of time. My substitutions: sweet onion instead of spring onion; crushed red pepper instead of chiles; omission of lime or substitute; and labne instead of yogurt. 1/4 pound of halibut pp, which is so expensive these days, was enough. Excellent recipe.

Superb. Tastes like Sicily and the sea. I used a short twisted pasta instead of bucatini, panko bread crumbs, and fennel fronds instead of tarragon. It needed little water at the end,l I think it was 2 shot glasses full. I really enjoy the recipes that use mostly pantry staples and this one is a true winner.

comments.recipe_name: green goddess salmon with potatoes and snap peas
comments.comment: Delicious and somewhat unusual. The sauce will vary depending on the herbs you use. I added parsley, dill, cilantro, chives, and mint and a little leftover pesto to full-fat labne (Lebanese yogurt). It was too thick to blend so I added some olive oil. For vegetables, I blanched both snap peas and asparagus and served those with the roasted potatoes and cucumber. My salmon took a little longer to cook as it was a nice thick piece. This is a nice spring dish when these herbs are in the garden.

comments.recipe_name: grilled swordfish with smoky tomato anchovy salsa
comments.comment: Fantastic. All I could want in a summer dinner. Delicious combination of flavors with very fresh swordfish (the quality of the fish makes all the difference). Cooked over a gas grill until done. Jalapeno instead of Fresno; fresh oregano instead of dried;a fresh salad instead of just arugula with a vinaigrette.

comments.recipe_name: baked orzo with tomatoes roasted peppers and zucchini
comments.comment: This is a wonderful,attractive, easy-to-like dish. What's best is that it can be made ahead, reheated or not, and varied depending on what's available.

comments.recipe_name: orecchiette with corn jalapeno feta and basil
comments.comment: A nice summer dish when corn is in season. I reduced the amount of feta and jalapeno and added sun-dried tomatoes. This would be good as a side dish to a more savory main.

Superb! I pretty much made as directed and found the flavor of the salmon and sauce delicious. I cooked the salmon in a small glass dish lined with parchment paper after marinating at room temperature for 15 minutes. The salmon needed a few more minutes to cook through--maybe 15 minutes in all--and it was wonderful. I served it with the rice with dill as suggested and drizzled the rice with the extra sauce.

Excellent. I soaked the rice for 10 minutes, then drained it, and put it in the pot with water and butter. I cooked it until the water was absorbed (about 10 minutes), stirred in the dill, covered the pot with a towel and then the lid, and then let it sit until dinner--inn this case the wonderful roasted dill salmon--about 1/2 hour with the stove turned off. I fluffed before serving and it was perfect. The rice was tasty and attractive.

comments.recipe_name: farro risotto with sweet corn and tomatoes
comments.comment: I really enjoy making and eating farro and this dish was an excellent way to use it. I think of farro as a winter grain but tonight I used it in mid-summer with a piece of grilled salmon. The flavors--the tomato and lemon--worked very nicely with the salmon. And the corn looked so pretty with it all (and using the cobs is always a plus when you have luscious New Jersey corn).
I used this marinade recipe for venison chops, which were grilled after marinating about 6 hours. The result was excellent, tender and tasty.

When I first made ratatouille I would cook my vegetables separately and then combine. For many years now I've been cooking them all together. This recipe takes me back to that original ratatouille. And it's so easy roasting the vegetables in the oven on separate racks. I agree with many of you about adding another tomato or two but all of the vegetables can be different sizes and it will still be fantastic when everything is fresh. You can garnish this ratatouille with different herbs and capers
Simple and perfect. Good halibut is just so good! Per another cook's suggestion I served it with a variation of the eggplant salad and it went so well/

comments.recipe_name: spicy shrimp with blistered cucumbers corn and tomato
comments.comment: I halved this recipe for a side dish for two and it worked just fine, in fact, was delicious served with rice. I used more corn proportionately (it's in season and well, why not?), used a regular cucumber, and half a jalapeno pepper. I quite enjoyed the flavors.

comments.recipe_name: roasted zucchini with garlicky bread crumbs and mozzarella
comments.comment: The flavors are excellent and the zucchini sticks are fun to eat. I had a large but tender zucchini and my pieces weren't even but it didn't matter. A little messy but that didn't matter. Instead of the olives on top, I spread a knifeful of a jarred tapenade on each one. I put foil down on my baking sheet so it would be easier to clean.

comments.recipe_name: grilled corn and avocado salad with feta dressing
comments.comment: This wasn't the knockout I expected. I think it needed some color--like a few halved cherry tomatoes. The grilled corn was delicious but somehow the parts didn't add up to a great salad. I halved the recipe and perhaps I needed more dressing. Also, a more flavorful green such as arugula would add interest and flavor.

comments.recipe_name: chickpea salad with fresh herbs and scallions
comments.comment: Easy and delicious. For a crowd I suggest making at least 1 1/2 times the recipe. I used a full-fat Bulgarian yogurt (because I had it) but don't see why a Greek 0% fat wouldn't work as well. The mustard makes the dressing.

This was totally new to me but I took a chance on it when t was hosting a large gathering. It was an enormous success; they loved it and I had many requests for the recipe. My take: the flavors were delicious (fresh corn is in season) but it is messy to eat. I prefer a dip where you don't need a plate underneath for most times but if you're outside and messy doesn't matter, well, this is a true winner.

comments.recipe_name: baked greek shrimp with tomatoes and feta
comments.comment: Yummy. I served it atop a slab of a good bread so the sauce could just soak in. Needed a fork and knife. Excellent side dish particularly at the end of summer when the garden overflows with tomatoes.

Super dish when corn is just right (and abundant). Flavors and textures work well together. In response to someone who said preparing the shishitos was laborious, just line up a few across a cutting board and cut off the stems. Then cut crosswise. You do not have to seed them. It should only take a couple of minutes. Very nice side dish to a piece of grilled salmon.

Absolutely delicious! For the two of us, I used a cup of arborio rice and everything else pretty much stayed the same. For step 3 I added one cup of hot water (to which I had added 1 tsp of Better than Bouillon--I don't think it made a difference however), and then with step 4 I added another cup of water. It turned out perfectly. The flavors were wonderful. My tomatoes were juicy and I did not bother to remove the skins. And it did cook in a remarkably short time with virtually no fuss.

What to do with leftover prime rib? Well, this makes for a wonderful supper. I sauteed the vegetables (step 2) in a cast-iron pan, moved them to a side, added the cooked potatoes to the other side of the pan. squished half of them, and then formed a large "patty" with my hands right in that pan. That way it could go right into the oven after setting on the stove. Very tasty. I served it without the eggs.

comments.recipe_name: olive oil braised chickpeas and broccoli rabe
comments.comment: This was good but not great. The aroma did fill up my kitchen and I liked that (some may not). I prefer sauteed broccoli rabe to this.
