Very nice. And, yes, comfort food. I used honeycrisps and somehow I wanted a little more flavor. I wish I could recreate my mother's baked apples--what apples would she have used?

comments.recipe_name: farro salad with leeks chickpeas and currants
comments.comment: Loved the complexity of textures and flavors of this dish. I didn't have leeks so I caramelized onions. I love a dish you can make ahead!

comments.recipe_name: spiced seared eggplant with pearl couscous
comments.comment: Absolutely delicious with and without the yogurt. The flavor is spicy but not hot (I went easy on the cayenne). One medium eggplant was perfect. I served it with a roast duck and I imagine a roast chicken would also be wonderful with the dish. As a side dish this could serve 8. Would be excellent for a buffet. One note: it is very easy to burn the couscous when toasting it. I looked away for a couple of minutes and had to start all over again.
Very nice as a condiment (salad) to grilled chicken. Use whatever herbs you have around.

This was excellent. I followed the recipe using the double-layered tin foil and roasting in the oven. So pretty. My only comment is that in the oven there is no way to know when the salmon "starts to sizzle." I removed it after about 15 minutes. Interestingly, the package that "rested" the longest was the tastiest, as in a very short time the flavors just came together. Such a pretty presentation.

comments.recipe_name: romaine and radish salad with buttermilk lemon dressing
comments.comment: The dressing is a bit thin even with a full-fat buttermilk but very tasty. My salad was a CSA special: napa cabbage, two kinds of radishes, and hakurei turnips. The flavors went together beautifully.

First time using this recipe. I usually do the 5-fold no-knead challah. I cut this recipe in half and found it easy to make, and oh so dramatic to serve. I very much wish the the recipe was in grams as there are so many variables in measuring. My dough was actually dry with the 3 cups of flour and I had to add about 1/4 cup of water. The first rise was 1 1/2 hours. I pulled my strands tightly when forming the loaf and my bread was almost 6" high when done. Beautiful.
I loved the stuffing. If I were making this again I think I would serve a thick tomato sauce on the side or bake it in tomato sauce. My squid were large so I only needed one per person. Delicious.

Fantastic quick dessert. I used a 9" cast-iron pan. Turned out I only had about 1 1/2 lbs of Pink Lady apples and still the dessert filled the pan. I caramelized the apples, made the egg mixture in my Kitchenaid (using the whisk attachment but I don't think that should matter), poured it into the pan, and baked. I put it in a cooled oven few a minutes prior to serving. Delicious! Microwaved a piece for breakfast.
Excellent. I used a thick Lebanese yogurt and grated the cucumber. Held up very nicely for several hours before serving. Served with lamb.

comments.recipe_name: skillet chicken with white beans and caramelized lemon
comments.comment: Lovely flavors. I read through the notes and sauteed the kale in the fat before adding the cannellini beans (drained and rinsed). This worked very well. I didn't eat the lemon rinds but they imparted a wonderful flavor to the completed dish (and they were attractive). All in all a very nice, relatively simple dinner.

comments.recipe_name: tomato sauce with capers and vinegar
comments.comment: My last pound of summer tomatoes, grated (large holes) made a most delicious, and quick, tomato sauce. The texture was thick and lustrous. Served it with sauteed mushrooms. Can't wait until next summer!

Delicious, attractive, and easy. I ground the spices and crusted the skinned fish as directed. I was very pleased with the results. I fried the fish in a combination of butter (not clarified) and avocado oil and served it with lemon wedges. Interesting combination of spices and the combination works.

comments.recipe_name: pumpkin bundt cake with maple brown butter glaze
comments.comment: Delicious addition to the Thanksgiving table. I cut down on the amount of sugar (367 grams) and I think it was perfect. Not too sweet but sweet enough. The glaze is quite sweet. And the cake looked picture perfect. I did need to add a little water to the glaze to get it to drip over the sides (put wax paper underneath as you do that and then pull it out. Also batter is spooned, not poured, into the prepared pan. Wonderfully moist and beautiful cake.

These were very good. I suggest putting the dough in the refrigerator for 5-10 minutes before forming the cookies. The batter will be much less sticky. And the Silpat cookie sheet liners work so well for a recipe like this. I got 36 good-sized cookies from this recipe and could easily have stretched it further.

comments.recipe_name: sweet spiced mushroom and apricot pilaf
comments.comment: Delicious dish but let me tell you know it changed overnight (and then reheated). The flavor of the star anise took over! The texture of the rice was perfect--not too soft--and the flavors were good but intense. I had used broken star anise, not whole, and perhaps that is what accounted for the change as I couldn't remove it all. I'll make it again because I really liked all those mushrooms but I'll remove the anise. Also, to get the basmati rice to cook more evenly, soak it first.
Very nice supper with a salad and bread. I used about 1/2 pound of orecchiette with about 3/4s of the recipe and it was luscious.

comments.recipe_name: osso buco with orange herb gremolata
comments.comment: Delicious winter dinner. I used beef shins from a local farm (I sure wish I understood all the cuts) and cooked them in the oven for a long time at a lower temperature. Served with polenta with mushrooms. I didn't have any olives and didn't miss them.

As a side for roast chicken, for two of us, I halved the recipe and cooked it all on one sheet pan, the butternut squash on one half of the pan. Delicious. Next time I'd add more onion and some garlic cloves. I turned the oven off after about 20-25 minutes and kept the vegetables warm while I finished preparing dinner. I love that this is what I call a minimum mess dish; just toss the foil and the sheet pan is clean.

comments.recipe_name: pasta with kale pesto and roasted butternut squash
comments.comment: After my significant other argued with me about the definition of pesto (I had committed a grave sin by calling this kale pesto), we enjoyed this dish and I'd definitely make it again if I found myself with a supply of kale and butternut squash. It was tasty and very attractive. The flavors had an intensity that you don't get from basil and each stood out. Delicous.

Spectacularly good. I had my fish man skin a 3/4 pound of swordfish (that's the right amount in my house for 2--with sides) and cut it into 3/4 inch slabs. I used canola oil (my bottle of grapeseed oil smelled off so out it went) and basically followed the instructions. I deglazed with a good splash of white wine after adding the shallots and then added the capers, which I had soaked in the lemon juice. I swirled in about 2 TBSP of butter. Really delicious!!

So easy and so good. I used a can of cannellini beans and a can of fava beans, and served with cilantro, sour cream, and the pickled onions. What a wonderful lunch when I had unexpected company. And it looked so pretty.

Delicious and easy. As for sticking, use the nonstick cooking spray. I had no problem at all: it slid right out of the pan. Nice texture, complex flavor, and not too sweet. A winner. I made it for the first night of Hanukkah. This cake could serve a crowd and be served with strawberries and cream if desired.

comments.recipe_name: spicy white bean stew with broccoli rabe
comments.comment: Used as a side dish to pan-grilled salmon. Looked and tasted great. Used kale, 1 can of cannellini beans, omitted the parsley/cilantro and egg, topped with plenty of feta cheese. I usually ad-lib. It's the structure and method of a recipe that are important. The recipe is the inspiration and I go from there. That may take years of cooking for some but as they say, relax. If you're working with good ingredients, it will be good.

Wonderful dish, filling, and quite beautiful over polenta. I used a mix of supermarket mushrooms and really any will do. The same goes for the wine. Ask into a wine store and ask for a wine you can use for cooking and drinking, i.e., relatively inexpensive. A salesperson will always be helpful.

comments.recipe_name: crispy parmesan roast chicken with lemon
comments.comment: Delicious, flavorful roast chicken. I just used a little bit of salt for the overnight marinade and then brought the chicken back to room temperature before roasting. The pan juices were wonderful. Adding the Parmesan at about halfway was really interesting and worked beautifully. Delicious with mashed potatoes.

comments.recipe_name: sheet pan chicken with sweet potatoes and peppers
comments.comment: Exceptional. I had low expectations as my husband insists he doesn't like sweet potatoes. However, he was gifted a 3-pounder. For this recipe I used t about a pound of the sweet potato, doubled the onion and garlic, and pretty much followed the rest of the recipe. The cilantro was omitted as I didn't have any. The timing was perfect (have the chicken at room temperature) and do use bone-in, skin-on chicken (Bell & Evans is good). The onion condiment tasted great and was pretty.

comments.recipe_name: sheet pan harissa salmon with potatoes and citrus
comments.comment: Lovely and easy dinner. I used lemon instead of orange and added cut-up sweet potato and fennel to the vegetable mix (my sheet pan was filled, unlike the one in the photo). My salmon fillets were relatively thick so I actually roasted them skin side to the side. They were in the oven 12 minutes. I omitted the cilantro. What I most liked about this recipe was that I could do the vegetables ahead and then put the fillets in their place and roast later. The marinade (20 min) added great flavor.

comments.recipe_name: farro salad with roasted rutabaga ricotta salata and hazelnuts
comments.comment: This was very nice. And I'm one who doesn't usually use rutabaga. Sweeter than most of my salads but very yummy. I used a combination of lettuces and napa cabbage for the greens.

comments.recipe_name: pasta e ceci italian pasta and chickpea stew
comments.comment: Excellent basic recipe especially good if you're having vegetarians over unexpectedly. I too added more water at the end and didn't drain the tomatoes. Very tasty and filling and attractive. With bread this can be a meal.

Easy, quick dinner. I found that the proportions and timing was spot on. I used a good fish stock (from Metropolitan Seafood in Lebanon NJ where I bought my excellent shrimp and they always label where it is from) and with a salad and a loaf of bread this was a great dinner (I'm still tasting the garlic, which to me is a good thing). For two people, I made the full amount of orzo with 12 oz of shrimp (unpeeled weight).

Very pleasing soup. Flavors were mellow. I used one large potato, low-salt chicken stock, and dried thyme. I had no problem getting the cabbage to caramelize on a gas stove in an All-clad Dutch oven with med-high heat. When I could see that the bottom of the pot was starting to turn brown I stirred. No residue on the pot and the cabbage and leek combo was excellent. The Parmesan cheese added another dimension. This was a delicious, comforting soup.

comments.recipe_name: creamy white beans with herb oil
comments.comment: Quite nice as a side dish to roast chicken or salmon. I used pesto leftover from last summer to top it. Very tasty and easy.

comments.recipe_name: wine braised chicken with artichoke hearts
comments.comment: This was an excellent, easy week-night dinner but good enough for company. However, I think, that you should make two thighs per person and have some leftover rather than not have enough. For the two of us I had 1.5 pounds of chicken (4 thighs) with the two cans of artichokes plus a light soup, salad, and a side dish.

comments.recipe_name: crisp gnocchi with brussels sprouts and brown butter
comments.comment: Very attractive dish and very tasty. I don't know if I've ever used store-bought gnocchi before and I found them too salty for my taste. But everyone else loved it. I cooked the brussels sprouts over rather high heat and thought I'd burned them when I went to stir but the kind of burntness just added to the flavor.

comments.recipe_name: radicchio salad with crunchy shiitake mushrooms and barley
comments.comment: Delicious salad. I suggest using Bob's Red Mill hull-less barley; it takes a little longer to cook but it has a fuller flavor. I loved this salad although I had to use regular mushrooms instead of shiitake and I feel there was too much radicchio for the mix. The layering was a wonderful idea, the colors were vibrant, the dressing and herbs on top added so much flavor. I served it as a side to roast chicken but this could also be a lunch by itself.

Absolutely delicious dish. I pretty much followed the recipe, using 3 cans of rinsed pinto beans. I added a couple of cups of water during step 3, not wanting the mixture to stick to the bottom of my pot and then added some more water when I reheated it the next day. I found the melange of flavors perfect. It was a huge hit and serves a small crowd for lunch over rice.

comments.recipe_name: skillet chicken with black beans rice and chiles
comments.comment: Wonderful, flavorful dinner. I poured off some fat after browning the chicken and I soaked the Basmati rice for an hour before cooking. After assembling the dish and adding the chicken stock I brought the pan (a 10-inch Staub) to a boil before putting it in the oven. Forty minutes later it was all cooked perfectly. I don't usually use hot peppers but two seeded jalapenos were excellent in this dish. Next time I would omit the cinnamon. I loved that the various components could be prepared ahead.

comments.recipe_name: white bean piccata pasta with broccoli
comments.comment: Very nice as a side dish. I used the full amount of ingredients for 1/2 pound of pasta and I still could have used more broccoli. Next time garlic and maybe Parmesan to finish with less lemon.

Fabulous. I made it with most of the ingredients. Yes, check at 20 minutes. For two I used all the vegetables with a cup of couscous (and water, not vegetable stock) and will have enough for a side tomorrow.
I separated most of the bok choy leaves for cleaning. This also made it easier to cover with the oil. Served it with a whole fish. Very tasty.

comments.recipe_name: slow roasted fish with mustard and dill
comments.comment: Is it possible to give this recipe 10 stars? Absolutely delectable. The whole porgy weighed 1 1/2 pounds. I was quite skeptical about this method and kept it in the oven at 200 for 70 minutes until I got the courage up to remove the fish. The directions were perfect--the bones just lifted out. And the sauce was divine (I used yogurt). Porgies are cheap and local and underutilized. Wow, wow, wow!! Good enough for the fanciest of fish-eating company.

Turned out perfectly. I grated the potatoes (2 lbs) and onion in my food processor, got as much water out as possible,added 3 eggs, heated a 7" skillet in the oven with the fat, removed the pan, put mixture in, and cooked for a few minutes on stove until sides browned. I then baked the kugel for about 20 minutes and removed from oven until about 1/2 hour before serving. Back in the oven until browned and heated through. Delicious. Turned out on a round platter, beautiful. Cut into wedges.

comments.recipe_name: lemony asparagus salad with shaved cheese and nuts
comments.comment: Delicious. Kind of a misnomer to call it a salad if you serve the asparagus warm. For herbs I used parsley, sage, chives, and a few sorrel leaves. For the cheese I used crumbled feta and I used pistachios roasted stove top in a skillet. I mixed all the herbs with the dressing. I loved the textures and flavors and thought it worked very well as a side dish to a veal chop. It might be a little too much to serve with salmon but maybe not. Certainly with any grilled meat it will be wonderful.
Delicious. I loved the hint of heat at the end. I cooked them a day ahead and reheated in the microwave. I used the chicken fat because I had it but another time would use butter.

Loved this. For the two of us, I used 1.5 pound of peeled shrimp, the full 15 oz can of cannellini beans, and one leek. I used fish stock for the liquid. This was absolutely delicious served on pieces of a toasted baguette, which I smothered in the beans and leeks and put one shrimp in the middle. This would make a wonderful first course. I think the leeks were essential to this dish as they stay relatively intact and make the dish look and taste great.;

comments.recipe_name: spring minestrone with kale and pasta
comments.comment: Very nice soup. Mine was so thick with vegetables it was almost a vegetable stew. I used asparagus, zucchini, peas, spinach, and whatever else was around. Excellent the second day too.

Fantastic recipe. I was lucky enough to have buffalo short ribs, which have much less fat and well, also taste delicious. I made them a day ahead, skimmed off what little fat there was, and reheated. I should have just warmed them at 325 and before serving or removed the ribs before reducing the liquid at 425. The sauce was wonderful. I recommend placing a round of parchment paper between the ribs and the pan lid before putting in the oven for the long braise.

For the two of us, I used two medium-sized peppers, 1/2 pound ground lamb, about about 3/4 cup Rao's marinara instead of the can of tomatoes. Tasted great (topped with some grated pecorino locatelli). Served with a small portion of spaghetti lightly coated with more marinara and sauteed mushrooms. Peppers held together and were cooked.
I just made the lamb meatballs, not the glaze. Small balls, about 5-6 minutes about 6" from broiling unit (on high). They were delicious.
What more can I say: my first bunch of asparagus of the season. Divine. Used parsley and chives because that's also what's in the garden now.

comments.recipe_name: lamb necks braised in wine with peppers
comments.comment: I made this again but this time with a lamb neck roast. The neck had been boned and it was bound with string, like many other roasts. It was a bit over 2 pounds. I marinated it over night and cooked it for about 2 1/2 hours (with parchment paper under the lid of my Dutch oven to seal in the juices), cooled it, reduced the juices by a bit, sliced the roast (thickly), and added the slices back to the pot on the stove to reheat. It was utterly delicious and elegant on mashed potatoes and peppers.

comments.recipe_name: farro and green bean salad with walnuts and dill
comments.comment: Delicious. I suggest toasting the walnuts in a cast iron pan prior to cutting them up. I substituted basil for the dill but other than that pretty much followed the recipe. I did cook the string beans in the frying pan as I prefer them softer. Flavors and textures were wonderful. I put the left over salad in the next night's green salad and it livened it up. Very nice.

comments.recipe_name: quick tomato white bean and kale soup
comments.comment: Very good. Another "kitchen sink" soup. And it is better the second day. I used a white turnip instead of the potato (a nice tiny crunch), one cup of leftover chicken broth (the rest water), didn't bother with the bouquet garni (just added herbs and removed bay leaf before serving). Used a 19 oz can of white kidney beans. Filling and tasty.

comments.recipe_name: greek baked fish with tomatoes and onions
comments.comment: Absolutely delicious. I used a piece of grouper just under a pound for the two of us, a 14-oz can of diced tomatoes, lots of onion and garlic, omitted the cinnamon, and only a few sprigs of parsley. Baked for about 20 minutes and served it with a bulgur pilaf (with onions and garlic). Hardy but light at the same time. With a salad, a lovely spring meal.

comments.recipe_name: sheet pan paprika chicken with tomatoes and parmesan
comments.comment: Delicious, easy, and pretty on a plate. I had two whole chicken legs, which I cut into drumstick and thigh. Put everything into a cast-iron frying pan and roasted. Served over couscous. If the chicken is at room temperature, it should be ready in 30 minutes but I always use a thermometer to check (it was ready). I shook the pan at about the halfway point. Basically, no clean-up.

comments.recipe_name: striped bass or mahi mahi with fennel leeks and tomatoes
comments.comment: This was wonderful, tasted like the Bahamas. I used about 3/4 lbs of mahi-mahi for the two of us, one fennel bulb, one leek, plenty of garlic, and the full 14-oz can of diced tomatoes. Served with roasted potatoes and a green salad. The fish was perfect, certainly not bland as it absorbed the flavors of the sauce.

comments.recipe_name: broccoli and scallions with thai style vinaigrette
comments.comment: Very nice. Roasting broccoli like this is just so easy and the result is very tasty. The sauce complemented it well.

OMG. This was good. I too used some Old Bay seasoning but I don't think it mattered. We used canola oil. It does sputter so be careful. Too bad soft shells are so expensive (I paid $10 apiece and we ate 2 each). I served it with orzo with asparagus recipe from NYT and it also had a crunch and lemon. Yum

comments.recipe_name: lemony orzo with asparagus and garlic bread crumbs
comments.comment: I liked the texture the bread crumbs gave to this dish, which is otherwise pretty bland. Next time, more garlic. Very nice accompaniment to fish.

comments.recipe_name: roasted cauliflower with lemon brown butter
comments.comment: Very nice way to cook cauliflower. I spread the florets onto a parchment-paper-covered sheet pan, poured the oil over them, and tossed. I think the water made a difference. Very tasty with the butter and sage. I cooked one good-sized cauliflower and used the few left-over florets in a salad the next night.

comments.recipe_name: brussels sprouts and roasted winter squash hash
comments.comment: For 2 of us I cooked 1/2 cup of black rice. I roasted in 2 separate cast-iron skillets 1/2 pound each of cut-up butternut squash and sprouts cut in half, adding garlic cloves and salt and pepper to each pan. I cooked them at 375. The sprouts took about 15 minutes, the squash 10 minutes longer. I served all three components in a bowl with the rice in the middle. The dish looked great and the flavors worked well together. Served it as a side dish to grilled fish.

comments.recipe_name: red pepper rice bulgur or freekeh with saffron and chile
comments.comment: This was very good. I used freekah (my first time using it) and it does take longer to cook but I loved the texture (similar to bulgur). The saffron was a surprise and works nicely but I could see playing with flavors on this dish depending on what you're serving it with.

This was a great side. I shredded the Brussels sprouts in the Cuisinart, didn't use the mint, but otherwise followed the recipe. I think it was a Gala apple; I shredded that too. Quick and easy. I served it with fried chicken. The next night I used it as slaw for tacos.

comments.recipe_name: stir fried swiss chard and red peppers
comments.comment: A nice, easy, and particularly attractive way to cook Swiss chard. I didn't have any ginger so I added a spice mix that contained ginger at the end, and served it as a side dish to fish (rather than over a grain). I used a minced shallot instead of the scallions. As for the chard, the easiest way to cut it is to stack a few leaves together, roll them, and cut. For under1/2 pound of chard, I made about 2/3s of the soy sauce mix.

comments.recipe_name: one pan shrimp and pearl couscous with harissa
comments.comment: A quick, easy, and very attractive dinner. Very tasty too. I don't like hot-hot and I was able to get just the right amount of spice to the dish. I used 2 medium-sized tomatoes instead of the cherry tomatoes. For the two of us, I used 12 oz of shrimp and 3/4 cup of couscous. With a salad this was a nice dinner. This dish would also work as a first course.

Delicious. I used a Vialone Nano rice. For two of us, I used 3/4 cups of rice and went from there, using about three cups of a stock-water combo. I decided not to add the lemon at the end and threw in some earthy spices and it was wonderful. Served under a rack of lamb. Very pretty and easy.

comments.recipe_name: baked barley risotto with mushrooms and carrots
comments.comment: Very nice. I really enjoyed the earthiness of this dish. And very attractive. I used Bob's Red Mill "hill-less" barley. After 50 minutes in the oven there was still some water in the pan, which I spooned out, and the barley was quite chewy, which I quite liked. Flavors were great.

comments.recipe_name: braised chicken thighs with greens and olives
comments.comment: Nice, attractive, and tasty supper. I used whole chicken legs, which I separated into thighs and drumsticks, shallots, Swiss chard, red-pepper flakes, and omitted the raisins. The chicken drumsticks needed an additional 10 minutes to cook, which did not hurt the thighs as there was plenty of liquid. The flavors worked very well together. I served it over barley. Delicious.

This worked perfectly for me and indeed rescued 1 1/2 pounds of "baby golds" that were on the soft side. After cutting them in half (not peeling) I cooked them in a Staub pan, used the olive oil, lemon, and chicken stock, and added some sumac, turned them as directed (added a touch more stock--I was so afraid they would stick as so many readers reported) and roasted them for about an hour. Delicious.

comments.recipe_name: roasted halibut with lemons olives and rosemary
comments.comment: Well besides the fact that I completely forgot about the olives, this was an excellent dinner. For two of us (with potatoes, asparagus, and a green salad as sides) I roasted 3/4 of a pound of halibut in one piece with the Aleppo pepper, lemon slices, and rosemary. Flavor was excellent. I did put my oven on broil after cooking for about 10 minutes for another 2-3 minutes.

comments.recipe_name: sugar snap peas with yogurt feta and dill
comments.comment: Absolutely wonderful. I used fresh, very young peas, radishes, and fennel fronds for the garnish. Looked gorgeous.

comments.recipe_name: grilled flank steak with worcestershire butter
comments.comment: I made this with a bison flank steak and it was tender and tasty. I added some crushed juniper to the marinade and did not make the tomato salad, instead serving it with a lentil and orzo stew with eggplant. The Worcestershire butter was delicious. Lovely.

comments.recipe_name: lentil and orzo stew with roasted eggplant
comments.comment: I thought this was delicious and recommend serving it as a side dish with something full-flavored. We had it with a flank steak and it went very well (it would overpower fish.) I cooked it through step 4--the lentils--and as I was finishing preparing dinner I added maybe a 1 1/2 cups water and the orzo. I quite loved the complexity of the flavors.

comments.recipe_name: salad e shirazi persian cucumber tomato and onion salad
comments.comment: Refreshing and attractive. I used an English cucumber and omitted the mint. Lovely summer salad.

comments.recipe_name: roasted salmon with miso rice and ginger scallion vinaigrette
comments.comment: This was a good, quick dinner. I liked it well enough but the presentation didn't wow me.

comments.recipe_name: yellow beet salad with mustard seed dressing
comments.comment: Delicious and gorgeous. I used brown mustard seeds and prepared horseradish. I will go out of my way this summer to get yellow beets to do this over and over.

Great idea to char the onions (I did them on the stove because it was very hot today). Added fresh thyme and oregano (any fresh herbs would work). Fish cooked evenly on the grill. Served the fish whole on the table, not filleted with lemon on the side and the onions on top. Delicious!
Perfect. Local lamb cops schmeared with a garlic-rosemary-sage-salt and pepper rub; marinated for a couple of hours; cooked to perfection. Topped with charred onions. So simple, so good.

comments.recipe_name: pearl couscous with shrimp and clams
comments.comment: Gorgeous dinner. I had my fish store clean and devein the shrimp and it still took me 1 1/2 hours to put this dish together. Yes,worth it! I used the shrimp shells, no heads (not available). The flavors were complex and satisfying. There was nothing difficult about the recipe, just many discrete steps, but it is long for a weeknight but these days.... The comments prompted me to taste Pernod alongside Ouzo and Anisette. Anisette has an unctuous sweetness; Ouzo is harsh; Pernod is just right.

comments.recipe_name: roast chicken with lemon and zaatar
comments.comment: Fantastic! I schmeared the chicken with yogurt in the morning, putting it in a zip-lock bag so I could caress it during the day; brought it to room temperature (about 1 1/2 hrs before going into the oven); made the za'atar mix (much more fragrant than store-bought); applied it; and put the chicken into the oven. Roasted 25 min. at 400, 30 min. at 350. Rested 10 min. Just delicious. Juicy, gorgeous, and so flavorful.

comments.recipe_name: steamed clams with garlic parsley butter and leeks
comments.comment: A wonderful summer dinner. I only used half of a stick of butter for 24 clams and the broth was rich and flavorful. Grilled slices of bread to mop up the juices.

This was fun, such a stretch for me, and so delicious. Used cornichons for the pickles. I had some problem with making the sweet kombu as I am not familiar with kombu and bought what the supermarket had: seasoned seaweed sheets. They disintegrated in the boiling water so instead I hydrated the dried seaweed flakes and that worked perfectly. What a treat. It's all about flavor and texture, not necessarily the "right" ingredients.

comments.recipe_name: chicken soup with toasted farro and greens
comments.comment: Easy and versatile. I loved the crunch of the farro! I used 3 chicken thighs, skin intact, and cut them up for serving. I did not remove the bones. This gives it more flavor. I imagine that to reheat tomorrow the soup will need some water added. Very filling and delicious.

comments.recipe_name: pan roasted corn and tomato salad
comments.comment: I sauteed the onion and corn in about 2 TBSP of butter and used red pepper flakes instead of the chilies. Wonderful salad. My corn (Hunterdon County in season) was sweet and this tasted like summer. Delicious salad.

comments.recipe_name: orecchiette with swiss chard red peppers and goat cheese
comments.comment: A great combination of flavors. I used small chunks of feta cheese and liked that the cheese stayed intact. Very pretty, easy, and delicious.
Perfect. I too used the solids to saute my vegetable side dish.

comments.recipe_name: soft shell crabs with spiced cucumber salad
comments.comment: I made the cucumber salad as a side to grilled soft shells. It was yummy. Substituted red pepper flakes for the chili paste and used basil instead of mint. Very nice.

comments.recipe_name: farro salad with corn and crispy chickpeas
comments.comment: Farro salads are just so good and this one is no exception. The crispy chickpeas are inspired (I had to hide them as someone in the house kept helping himself to them). Watch the corn--it goes to scorched rather quickly. And let the salad sit a bit before serving to let the flavors mellow. Tasty and attractive, not difficult although it has many steps, and the many textures make it a keeper.

comments.recipe_name: farro salad with corn and crispy chickpeas
comments.comment: Another wonderful farro salad, this one with varied textures and flavors. The crispy chickpeas are inspired. Watch the corn as it can burn quickly. And let this salad sit for a bit before you finish it with the chickpeas--it needs to mellow and be re-seasoned. A bunch of steps but not difficult and the payoff is very tasty.
Superb. Used a 12 oz salmon filet (skin on), marinated for 3 hours in about 3/4s of the glaze recipe and then grilled. Absolutely wonderful. Will try marinating even longer next time.

comments.recipe_name: spicy cucumbers with yogurt lemon and herbs
comments.comment: I loved this with fresh, crisp cucumbers. Next time I'd cut them into 1-inch pieces. I used fresh oregano instead of the mint. Still good the next day at lunch.

A tasty and quick dinner. I upped the amount of shrimp and squid, used panko bread crumbs, and quickly pan-toasted the canned chick peas. Flavors were delicious and spinach almost disappears into the stew.

comments.recipe_name: spicy eggplant salad with sesame oil
comments.comment: Easy, can be made in advance, and will be good the next day. I steamed about 10 very small Japanese eggplants, omitted the ginger and cilantro. Added a jalapeno and basil. Delicious. Great on crackers.

Easy and delicious. Served with vanilla ice cream instead of the lemon whipped cream and a glass of Port. I pitted one pound of sweet cherries for this recipe and pretty much followed as written. I couldn't taste the black pepper but it must have cooled down the sweetness of the cherries. Check the cake at 20 minutes for this baked quickly. No problem inverting the cake from a cast-iron pan.

comments.recipe_name: spiced lamb chops with fennel and cucumber
comments.comment: Cooked the lamb chops on the grill. Salad flavors (fennel, cucumber (I used a regular cucumber) and lemon) with the chops are unexpected and quite nice. I basically used salad as a salsa.

comments.recipe_name: summer shrimp scampi with tomatoes and corn
comments.comment: Wonderful dinner. I served it over 1/2 pound thin spaghetti. When the corn and tomatoes are fresh, and you have access to good shrimp, nothing could be better. For 2, used 3/4 pound of shrimp and 3 large ears of corn. More than enough.
I basically made matchstick carrots and they were a wonderful side to a casual dinner. I didn't use any parsley. Attractive and the sweetness was nice with the skate I served as the main attraction.
Delicious salad. Used basil instead of cilantro. Bought frozen edamame in pods and took a bit more time to assemble but worth the effort for the diverse textures and attractive presentation.

comments.recipe_name: pasta with caramelized cabbage anchovies and bread crumbs
comments.comment: Wonderful. I made it as a side dish for 2. Having read through all the notes I decided to use 6 cups of cabbage and 6 oz of pasta. It was more than enough. I had no trouble cooking the the cabbage down in 10-15 minutes (in a Staub cast-iron pan it's quick) but I had the time so just cooked it low and slow for an hour (covered to hold in the moisture). Flavors were excellent.

comments.recipe_name: cold pink borscht in a glass
comments.comment: Very enjoyable. I prefer adding sour cream.

comments.recipe_name: sheet pan chicken with roasted plums and onions
comments.comment: Delicious and easy. My very ripe plums disintegrated into a complex, balanced sauce. I served it over polenta flavored with pesto.

comments.recipe_name: tsimmes beef carrot and sweet potato stew
comments.comment: Excellent. I served it over egg noodles. I browned the meat and then brought it to a boil with the water prior to putting in the oven. I used both sweet and regular potatoes and didn't find the dish sweet. I do suggest that the broth be reduced and thickened, by making a roux or adding Wondra flour.

Fantastic idea to glaze the scallops with miso and mirin. We grilled 10 scallops with this wonderful sauce. I forgot about the sesame seeds and parsley and served them over Ottolenghi's turmeric rice, to which I added fresh corn and omitted the parsley. What a dinner!

Delicious and so pretty. Put grilled scallops on top. Flavors are so nice. For 2 I used 1 cup of basmati rice and scaled the recipe down. Added (just because I had it) about 1/2 cup of corn kernels and they added to the texture and beauty of the dish.

Easy and delicious. I omitted the cheese. I think adding tiny lamb meatballs to this soup and a touch of lemon might work very nicely. When working with escarole, make sure to clean it thoroughly. I drop the chopped greens into a large bowl of cold water and then scoop the greens out, leaving all the grit behind.

comments.recipe_name: roasted salmon with fennel and lime
comments.comment: A thick piece of salmon (about 14 oz for 2 of us) needed about 35 minutes to cook through and another 10 minutes wouldn't have hurt. Flavors were delicious and it was pretty. If I were doing this again I would probably precook the bottom layer of salmon before putting the salmon atop.
Bob's Red Mill soft white wheat berries give a great crunch. I soaked them for a couple of hours before cooking. Not sure that made any difference but the final result was very nice.

comments.recipe_name: linguine with clams roasted tomatoes and caramelized garlic
comments.comment: Delicious. Left out mint. I suggest using a good-sized saute pan for step 3--cooking the garlic, wine, and then clams--and adding the pasta to it (no need to remove clams before adding pasta). I added a little of the pasta water to the pan too. Very pretty dish and very delicious.

I used an old beef shin bone that I found in my freezer and really had no hopes of saving. It was spectacular cooked in my Cuisinart electric pressure cooker. The meat weighed about a pound (bone and all) but I followed the rest of the recipe. Enough for two with leftovers. The next time I see bone-in veal shanks I'm coming back for this recipe.

comments.recipe_name: roasted squash and radicchio salad with buttermilk dressing
comments.comment: Wonderful salad although my husband didn't like that I used the roasted squash seeds in it. My first time using delicata squash and if you can find them (in the fall at farmers' markets) seems like a no-brainer. My salad was composed of radicchio, napa cabbage, and some random greens. Dressing was yummy.
Excellent. I omitted both the dill and parsley.

Wowie zow. Absolutely marvelous and so easy. I will always have a package of chicken thighs in my freezer so I can make this dish whenever I need a quick dinner. Amazing! Starting from room temperature, bone-in, skin-on thighs took 30 minutes in the hot oven, time for me to make rice and saute some spinach. Amazing.

comments.recipe_name: short ribs in broth with squash and shiitakes
comments.comment: So good. Next time (and there sure should be a next time) I will put parchment under the lid of my Dutch oven and put the pot into the oven to cook at 325 for maybe 4 hours. Flavor was wonderful, just short ribs needed to be a bit more tender. I had a delicata squash but as they are not always available I imagine butternut would work as well. Does anyone know how to make this in an electric pressure cooker?

I made a batch to serve with steak. Added a large, sliced portobello mushroom to the mix. I enjoyed the cumin although I probably didn't use a full 2 tsp. Use any herb or spice depending on what you're serving this with, e.g., taggaron might be super with chicken.
This was a great side dish. Very versatile and easy.

Gorgeous. I had about 1/2 a fried chicken leftover so I took the meat off the bone (kept the crispy skin on) and progressed to #step 3. I used stone-ground white cornmeal. Biscuits turned out perfectly. Only thing I added to the filling was a 1/2 of frozen peas (for old-time sake). This made enough for dinner for two with enough left over for lunch. Really delicious and relatively easy. Would be dramatic in individual skillets with 2 biscuits apiece. Thanks for the gram measurements for biscuits

Beyond good. The cost of Nantucket bay scallops is astronomical but they were the best scallops I've ever had. I bought 3/4s of a pound of these tiny gems and dusted them lightly with flour as directed, cut up about 2 dozen cherry tomatoes, instead of using plum tomatoes, and used plenty of fresh garlic. Made 1/2 pound of spaghetti, which I put into the pot after thickening the sauce, added about a cup of pasta water, and then topped with the cooked scallops. Gorgeous dinner for 2.

comments.recipe_name: avocado salad with herbs and capers
comments.comment: Lovely idea. I used what I had: first a layer of thinly sliced radicchio, followed by a layer of sweet onion, and then pea shoots. Topped with the sliced avocado. Dressing was red wine, garlic, and olive oil. Capers sprinkled over the salad. Very pretty and pleasant.

Wonderful. I used "perlato Toscana" farro, mixed dried mushrooms, and fresh shiitake and portobellos. This was delicious, with or without the cheese and still good rewarmed two days later. Very versatile, very earthy.

comments.recipe_name: buttered stuffing with celery and leeks
comments.comment: Excellent. I used cubed bread that my supermarket sells for stuffing. This is a very standard stuffing that most everyone likes and sometimes the comfort-level is important. No reason not to put some of it in the turkey, in my opinion.

comments.recipe_name: gently cooked salmon with mashed potatoes
comments.comment: Wonderful dish. Very attractive and flavorful. My piece of salmon was rather thick (for 2 of us a 12 oz fillet) so I cooked it at 375 for about 15 minutes (and my husband likes salmon cooked through). Served over leftover mashed potatoes (from Thanksgiving), which I had refreshed with a couple of dabs of butter, The chive sauce was a winner and the plate looked like it came out of a professional kitchen. Served a side of roasted Brussels sprouts. Thank you all for your comments on temperature.

I too cut the Brussels sprouts in half before tossing them with olive oil and salt and pepper. Put them face down on parchment on a baking sheet spaced apart;turned after 10 minutes; cooked another 10-12 minutes. They were wonderful.

comments.recipe_name: skillet mustard chicken with spinach and carrots
comments.comment: Quite perfect. For two of us (and it was more than enough) 4 small thighs and all of the rest of the ingredients as written (except for the dill) were used. Not only was this gorgeous but it was so tasty. I served it over leftover Thanksgiving stuffing because that's what I had but couscous, rice, even mashed potatoes would work.

I came to this recipe with an open mind. My husband makes the very best latkes and I was hoping to impress. The flavor was good; I liked that the prep is done in advance; however, without a binder (eggs) the "latkes" are just not going to stay together. Flipping them was an ordeal. While there is never anything bad about fried potatoes and the bits that fell off where sublime, I think we'll stick to our regular latke (blood-letting) routine.

Delicious. Husband very skeptical; thought it would be too sweet. Not at all sweet. Complex. Only recommendation I would make is to use skinless salmon. The skin is not crispy and it's useless. I would oil the baking pan just a little before putting salmon in. For two, I used .8 pounds of salmon. Served with smashed potatoes and broccoli rabe. Oh, he likes well-done salmon, so I roasted it about 25 minutes. It was luscious.

comments.recipe_name: butchers steak with leafy greens salsa verde
comments.comment: Delicious. I had a bison hanger steak, about one pound. For the sauce I used one large leaf of Swiss chard (much of stem discarded), 4 scallions (2 in the sauce), and garlic. Cooked steak as directed and put it over the greens. Forgot about the lemon. Very attractive and delicious. Served with sauteed potatoes with onions and yellow pepper.

comments.recipe_name: crisp gnocchi with brussels sprouts and brown butter
comments.comment: Delicious and easy and very attractive. Flavor was outstanding. I cooked the Brussels sprouts until I thought they were done. Even in a 12" pan they were scrunched together so it took a little longer. I was skeptical about cooking the gnocchi this way and my husband thought I had "burned" them but it worked perfectly.

Rushed to make a side dish for dinner. Quickly boiled 3 oz of soba noodles, took them out of the pot into a colander with cold water running over it, boiled about 6 oz of frozen shelled edamame. Whisked in a salad bowl juice of 1/2 a lime, TBSP low-salt soy sauce, TBSP white miso, and 1/2 TBSP sesame oil (mistook it for mirin), and then about 4 oz baby spinach. Threw soba and edamame in bowl, added scallions. Delish.

So easy to throw together. (I used the Kitchen Aid.) Put it in the oven while you're having dinner and then serve warm with that dollop of ice cream. This is a perfect dessert for those who don't like overly sweet and/or elaborate desserts. Do use that toothpick.

comments.recipe_name: creamy mussel stew with peas fennel and lemon
comments.comment: I didn't have the creme fraiche so I don't know how that impacted the final product but this was good but not great. The mussels were excellent but that's due to my fishmonger. The fennel just didn't add enough pizzazz.

comments.recipe_name: baked and sauteed spaghetti squash on a bed of spinach
comments.comment: This is very good but a little strange visually: the squash sitting atop the spinach. I followed the recipe except for the walnuts because I felt their texture might distract. Roasting a whole spaghetti squash is easy. I doubled the bread crumbs as suggested and there still weren't enough to see them "color." They did add some body and the flavor was nice. Might be interesting to use flavored brad crumbs. Used as a side to a salmon fillet.

comments.recipe_name: arctic char with soba noodles pine nuts and lemon
comments.comment: Lovely, light dinner. For 2, I used 3/4 pounds of Arctic char and 6 oz of noodles with all the sauce. To plate only used half the noodles and then we devoured the rest. Served with roasted rainbow carrots with a tahini glaze. Beautiful dinner. I used regular lemon and did not garnish with herbs. Really delicious.

So good I made this two nights in a row. As a side for two (for salmon/rack of lamb) about 3/4 lb. of rainbow carrots (from Wegman's). Didn't measure ingredients for glaze but more cayenne than I normally use for anything with the silan and both times it turned out wonderfully. Tahini from Seed + Mill makes such a difference. First time using silan and was skeptical, given we don't like sweet, but this just worked. Try this with salmon or Arctic char.

Fantastic and not onerous as so many of the comments led me to believe.The lamb meatballs were light, yet filling. I left out the cinnamon (my husband doesn't like it) and the cilantro (I didn't have any) and used panko for the bread crumbs. Served over plain pearl couscous and adorned with jammy roasted cherry tomatoes. This would be a delicious appetizer for a dinner party (if those ever exist again). The aromas in my kitchen were so inviting.

I used these for the North African Meatball recipe. I sliced a dozen or so cherry tomatoes in half and tossed with olive oil, cumin, paprika, and salt as directed. Roasted for about 20 minutes until they were jammy. Delicious with the meatballs and attractive piled on top.

I made this with bison. It was good, not great. I liked the mustard-flavored sauce but was annoyed that the carrots much longer to cook than noted in the recipe (luckily, I had read all the notes). Served over wide noodles the stew was a delicious dinner. I cooked one pound of meat with all the veggies and added more wine at the end.

Greatly enjoyed the story and the dinner I cooked. For the 2 of us, I used 2 whole chicken legs (Bell & Evans) and 3/4 cup of brown rice. I didn't want any leftover spice mix so this is what I did: 1/2 tsp of garlic and onion powders, coriander, and smoked paprika, and 1/8 tsp of cayenne. Only needed an hour in the oven plus final browning. Sour cream sauce delicious too.

comments.recipe_name: roasted brussels sprouts with honey and miso
comments.comment: Very nice. For about 12 oz of Brussels sprouts I used 1/2 TBSP red miso and 3/4 tsp of honey. Not too sweet in the least. Put the halved sprouts heads up on a 1/2 baking sheet covered with aluminum foil, roasted for about 15; turned the over and cooked maybe another 12 minutes. Nice flavor and texture.

comments.recipe_name: crisp braised duck legs with aromatic vegetables
comments.comment: Fantastic dinner. For the two of us I used 2 D'artagnan duck legs (dried and seasoned, overnight in the refrigerator), which do not have nearly as much fat as duck legs usually do. I seared them very well. I needed to add a little duck fat to the pan. Added two white turnips to the mix and cooked uncovered as directed. Did not take the meat off the bone. Pretended I was in a French bistro.

This is delicious. Having read all the comments, I added 2 cups of stock instead of 3 and that was enough. Tasty and a nice change. I used 0 % fat Greek yogurt; I think full-fat or even 2% would have been better. And I didn't have a 10x13 dish so I used one a little larger. Next time the slightly smaller one. 9x13.

comments.recipe_name: lemony white bean soup with turkey and greens
comments.comment: Used spinach and 1 pound of ground turkey. Very nice and even better the next day.

Excellent. For 2, I bought 3/4 lb large shrimp and served over white rice. More than enough for a wonderful dinner. Used a can of diced tomatoes and omitted the sugar. For the spice mix, as I only wanted to make only what I'd use here, I cut seasoning recipe by 1/3 but used all the vegetables and spices for the shrimp. The smoked paprika makes all the difference in the world and is reminiscent of New Orleans' flavors. Loved the topping also.

I used about 1 1/2 lbs of bison oxtail for this recipe. Basically cut the braising liquid in half and cooked in the oven 4-5 hours. Cooked and reheated the next day. Needed more liquid (threw in some wine and chicken broth), simmered for a while, and then served over medium-width egg noodles. Quite a delicious winter supper. The garnishes (gremolata and celery leaves) brighten the dish up.

Excellent. For 2 of us I used 1 lb of ground lamb and roughly 1/2 the spices and herbs. Omitted mint. Baked in a 8" loaf pan a bit short of the 90 minutes (used a thermometer). I was able to turn it onto a serving platter and I quite loved it. The sauce was good but not necessary, as the meatloaf was very juicy. I would make it again. Served it with a roasted, spiced eggplant; recipe from La Boite site.

comments.recipe_name: braised white beans and greens with parmesan
comments.comment: Very nice side dish. Tasty and filling. I used both the mozzarella and pecorino Romano and liked the flavor but found it a bit salty from the cheese. Can be made through step 3 and then briefly rewarmed and cheese added. Served with a juicy roast chicken.

comments.recipe_name: skillet chicken thighs with broccoli and orzo
comments.comment: Very nice, easy, one-dish dinner. I particularly liked the browned orzo. For 2 of us I used 4 small chicken thighs (maybe 1 1/2 pounds ) and 1 cup of orzo. Very tasty with one thigh leftover for lunch. I recommending not eating the lemon, so just push it to the side of the plate. It does add a nice flavor and is attractive.

comments.recipe_name: seared sea scallops with ginger lime butter
comments.comment: I didn't expect to like the scallops-sweet potato combination but they went beautifully together. The bok choy was ok but string beans or another green would have worked just as well. Dish was gorgeous. My scallops were smaller so timing needed to be adjusted but they turned out perfectly. This is indeed an elegant supper. Oh, and I loved the ginger-lime butter. I grated the ginger on a microplane and melted it all in the microwave instead of the pan.

Delicious. I won't pretend that I know from chili but whatever I made was darn good. Served over rice. Used what I had: a pound of ground bison, a bottle of non-alcoholic beer, coffee made from Medaglio d'Oro powder, a TBSP or so of light brown sugar, half a jalapeno, a squirt of Sriracha (instead of the chili sauce) and all the other listed ingredients. Just enough heat but not too hot. Plenty left over for tomorrow. Tasty, filling, and I had everything in the house.

Wonderful. Thank you to all the commentators as I am salt sensitive and when I realized my Cajun seasoning contained salt I knew not to add any more salt to anything. This was a delicious, quick dinner. You do, however, need a sharp knife to make those slits in the salmon. For 2 of us, one .80 lb. salmon fillet plus 1 lb of small potatoes, and 1 lb of large asparagus, bottom half shaved.

comments.recipe_name: lemony chicken feta meatball soup with spinach
comments.comment: Quite nice. Flavors and textures all work. Used ground turkey, turkey stock (from Thanksgiving), and 5 oz of frozen spinach, which I microwaved prior to putting into soup. The lemon at the end tied it all together.

I loved this. Made as directed except that I omitted the cinnamon and used a good-sized handful of cherry tomatoes (didn't peel) for the tomato. The timing is off as many other readers noted. Eggplant sat in colander for 30 minutes but I hadn't allotted time for step 5, sitting uncovered at room temperature. Sat for maybe 10. Delicious. Loved it with yogurt. Served it as a side for swordfish.

Traditional or not this was delicious. For 2, I used about 2 pounds of beef short ribs and about 3/4 of each of the other ingredients. Used white turnips instead of the daikon and a smaller amount of regular sugar. Cooked in the pressure cooker as directed in step 1 and then when cooled put meat and veggies in a bowl and refrigerated sauce and meat separately. Removed fat the next day and continued with the recipe. Served with rice and kimchi. Meat was so tender and luscious.
Delicious. Salmon was very light and flaky and the flavor was delicate but pronounced. Would be nice served over white rice.

comments.recipe_name: whole roasted cauliflower with pistachio pesto
comments.comment: Delicious. The pistachios lend a richness to the pesto that worked beautifully with the cauliflower and muted the cilantro (not a bad thing). I made this with salmon but next time I would use it as a side to something more formidable, I'm thinking lamb chops. And I'd make more of the pesto.

comments.recipe_name: white wine braised rabbit with mustard
comments.comment: OMG this was good. I used a 2.75 pound D'Artagnan rabbit that cost $30 and was worth every penny. I have local Pennsylvania Dutch rabbits available and they are much fattier and just not as tender. Before putting in the oven I covered the rabbit with parchment paper and then cooked for just over an hour, until the aroma just took over the kitchen. Served over pappardelle, highly recommended.

comments.recipe_name: sheet pan scallion chicken with bok choy
comments.comment: Cooking chicken without the skin is a no no where I come from. So: for the 2 of us I bought 3 thighs (about 1 1/2 lbs) and browned them both sides in a a little olive oil in a cast iron pan (Staub). I removed them, added the bok choy, which I mixed with the fat from browning the thighs, and then smothered the thighs with the mustard-miso combo before putting them atop the bok choy, put the pan in the oven, and 20 mins later 165 F. Served with rice mixed with mushrooms. Superb dinner.

comments.recipe_name: roasted asparagus with crispy leeks and capers
comments.comment: Is six stars possible? Lined the sheet pan with parchment so no clean-up. Very thick asparagus, about 22 minutes (I could smell the asparagus-leek mixture); squeezed a lemon on them and served with sauce. As good as it gets.

comments.recipe_name: tunisian grilled peppers and tomatoes with couscous
comments.comment: Great side dish. Ground caraway seeds in pestle and added a spice blend that had chile pepper in it, and garlic powder. Served warm as a side to a rib-eye steak (bison), forgot the olives, and was very pleased with the result. Just a touch of heat and that was perfect.

comments.recipe_name: spring pasta bolognese with lamb and peas
comments.comment: Loved this. For the two of us I made the entire recipe (using light cream instead of heavy) and 8 oz of pasta. Served with a wilted spinach salad. Enough bolognese sauce for a second dinner. May add some red pepper flakes tonight. Very delicious dinner. Sauce coated the pasta (a thick Italian spaghetti) perfectly.
Good on most anything! Hurrah for miso!

comments.recipe_name: white beans with radishes miso and greens
comments.comment: I didn't have high expectations for this dish--I thought it would be mushy. But this was delicious and an excellent side for grilled salmon. I used a combo of arugula and baby spinach with two cans of cannellini beans. The two of us ate it all. I kept the heat on low when I wilted the greens and added a bit more water. Delicious and easy.

So easy. And delicious. I can see a crowd eating these non-stop. Anyway, I marinated 10 "Buffalo" chicken wings (I don't know exactly what that means but that's what the Bell & Evans package said). Used about half the amounts of marinade, subbed rice cooking wine for the sake, lots of garlic, and then grilled after marinating for maybe 35 minutes. Delicious and addictive,

comments.recipe_name: pasta primavera with asparagus and peas
comments.comment: Very nice, anticipating spring, dish. Would be lovely as a small pasta course before an entree. I added about a cup of pasta water to the mix to lighten things up at the end, over a very low flame.

comments.recipe_name: italian red wine braised duck with olive gremolata
comments.comment: Quite delicious. D'Artagnan is now selling Rohan duck legs in packages of two, so perfect for us. Used about 2/3rds of recipe for the rest. Gremolata was excellent. As suggested I served lduck egs over buttered noodles to soak up the wonderful sauce. I might have added some fresh peas or had a side of fennel. Use your judgment as to when legs are tender.

comments.recipe_name: soy butter basted scallops with wilted greens and sesame
comments.comment: For the two of us, we had 8 beautiful dayboat scallops and 10 oz of spinach. Served over the rice (I added some sauteed mushrooms because I had them), this was a delicious and filling dinner.

comments.recipe_name: farro with blistered tomatoes pesto and spinach
comments.comment: This was good although not my favorite farro preparation. I felt that there was just too much going on in the dish. I omitted the cheese I don't know if that would have made a difference.

comments.recipe_name: grilled duck breast with miso ginger and orange
comments.comment: I turned this into a spring dinner for 2. One duck breast marinated over night. Salad: I added radishes, Vidalia onions, baby spinach, arugula, cabbage, whatever was around, to the mix. Cooked 3 oz of soba noodles. So noodles as base, salad (dressing was wonderful, added some olive oil and a bit more sesame oil to expand) and duck and mango on top. This would be a great summer dish around the pool. The duck looked burnt but was perfect, cooked over gas grill. More flames than usual but so good.

comments.recipe_name: orecchiette with cherry tomatoes and arugula
comments.comment: Visually beautiful. I used arugula and ramps (the bulb with the garlic and the leaves with the arugula) and topped with fresh basil and shaved Parmesan. This was a stunning side dish to a grilled veal chop. Delicious.

Fantastic recipe. For the two of us, I used four Bell & Evans chicken thighs (one left over for tomorrow's lunch), about 1/2 pound fresh carrots, 1/2 lb small potatoes, and about 1/4 lb asparagus (added about 10 minutes before rest of dish was done). All roasted on aluminum foil on sheet pan (no clean-up). I used 2 small avocados and half of a seeded jalapeno (can't handle hot) for the sauce. Finished plate with a few cherry tomatoes. Wonderful dinner!!

comments.recipe_name: black rice and red lentil salad
comments.comment: I liked all the components of this salad and all of the textures but didn't feel it added up to anything I wanted to eat again. Perhaps if I had dressed it an hour or so before serving and let it evolve. I used Venere black rice, which I leaned is a variant of the Chinese forbidden rice.

I made this with left-overs from a whole grilled chicken. I used about 3 oz of shredded chicken, 3 oz of sugar snap peas (freshly picked) and maybe 2/3s of the rest of the ingredients plus a bit more sesame oil just before serving. I had it for 2 small lunches. Even better the second day.
